Repository.Name: JetUML
Repository.Description: A desktop application for fast UML diagramming.

MDU6SXNzdWU0OTUyNTAzNDE=.Title: Support node viewing through stateless strategies
MDU6SXNzdWU0OTUyNTAzNDE=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU0OTUyNTAzNDE=.Body: Modify the design of the node viewers do that they follow a structure parallel to that used for edges, as implemented for #309.

MDU6SXNzdWUyNDE3NTIwMDI=.Title: Create separate Edge and Node packages
MDU6SXNzdWUyNDE3NTIwMDI=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWUyNDE3NTIwMDI=.Body: Move all edge-management code to a separate package `...graph.edges`

Comments.Body: This would make the tool backward-incompatible with previous versions for loading in graphs. Not worth it for now.
Comments.Author: prmr

MDU6SXNzdWU1MzY3MTU4MA==.Title: Update the style file to reflect the current code
MDU6SXNzdWU1MzY3MTU4MA==.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU1MzY3MTU4MA==.Body: However, don't overfit. Checks for some types of problems (like the use of raw types) should be left in, and we'll improve these as we go. If you have any doubt about what rules should be dropped or changed, post here.

Comments.Body: After a second look, it seems that it would not be such as bad thing to leave the current style file as is and work through the all the refactorings over time as we edit the code. I'll mark this as won't fix. Please activate checkstyle with the current style file.
Comments.Author: prmr

I_kwDOAblrlc5CBHCV.Title: Add macOS (Apple Silicon) release?
I_kwDOAblrlc5CBHCV.Author: jiahao-c
I_kwDOAblrlc5CBHCV.Body: Currently, there's only a macOS(x64) release. Can we also have a macOS (Apple Silicon) release, so that M1 Mac users can run JetUML more smoothly?

Comments.Body: Thanks for pointing this out. To ensure the integrity of the distribution, we currently can only offer self-contained binaries that are built by trusted contributors available at the time of release. But we will try to cast the net a bit wider for future releases, and hopefully include Apple Silicon.
Comments.Author: prmr

MDU6SXNzdWUxMTA1NTQyMzk=.Title: Multi-selection behavior not consistent with specification
MDU6SXNzdWUxMTA1NTQyMzk=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWUxMTA1NTQyMzk=.Body: When selecting edges with the multi-selection (Ctrl key), selecting an edge when a node is selected un-selects the node, but then it's possible to re-include it in the multi-selection by selecting it. It's also not possible to select multiple edges. The selection behavior needs to be cleaned up.

Comments.Body: At this point it's not possible to select edges inside a `PackageNode` or in an `ImplicitParameterNode`
Comments.Author: prmr

MDU6SXNzdWU0NTc2MTkzODU=.Title: Use UTF-8 encoding when saving diagrams
MDU6SXNzdWU0NTc2MTkzODU=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU0NTc2MTkzODU=.Body:
Diagrams are currently saved using the default system encoding, which can lead to ambiguity when sharing diagrams across system. Change the persistence service to explicitly encode/decode using UTF-8.

This change will impacted previously saved diagrams that were saved in a format other than UTF-8, and which contain special characters (such as the French quotes used for UML stereotypes). The workaround will be to externally change the encoding.

**Migration notes:** Any diagram saved with a version 2.1-2.5 that contains non-ASCII characters can be made compatible simply by changing the encoding with the help of a text editor. The easiest way to do this is to create a new file set with UTF-8 encoding, and paste the content of the old file in.

MDU6SXNzdWU1NTExNTI2NDA=.Title: Application crashing on window de-focus
MDU6SXNzdWU1NTExNTI2NDA=.Author: jkatofsky
MDU6SXNzdWU1NTExNTI2NDA=.Body:
**Description**
Often (but not always) when I click away from JetUML to focus on another window, JetUML will crash.

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Be working on a JetUML project
2. Click away from the JetUML window, changing focus to another.
3. Often, JetUML will crash.

**Configuration**
 - JetUML Release: 2.5
 - OS + version: macOS Catalina v. 10.15.3 
 - Java version 8, Update 141

Comments.Body:
Thanks for reporting. It seems that [MacOS Catalina is not a certified system config](https://www.oracle.com/technetwork/java/javase/certconfig-2095354.html).

See also: [Javafx crashes on OSX on JRE 1.8](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/34711128)

Could this explain the problem? Are you able to run of a certified config to check whether the problem persists? 

There are a lot of incompatibilities with Mac, I'm working on some documentation to clarify this.
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body: Related to: #300, #280
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body: Now subsumed by #324
Comments.Author: prmr

MDU6SXNzdWUyNDE3NTk4Nzk=.Title: Move draw method to separate hierarchy
MDU6SXNzdWUyNDE3NTk4Nzk=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWUyNDE3NTk4Nzk=.Body:
Move the `Edge.draw(...)` method out of the `Edge` interface and into a new interface `EdgeRenderer` in charge of drawing edges. Instead, edges should return their renderer.

Requirements:
* Some layouts require a global analysis of the graph.
* Some layouts allow multiple edges of the same types between nodes.
* Some layouts require knowing how many edges are attached to different sides of a node
* Layouts should be aware of the grid that they can snap to.
* The design should allow pluggable contributions (i.e. for people to add new layout algorithms).

Another desirable characteristic is for a design that allow incremental introduction of layouts into the existing code.

Comments.Body:
Initial draft.
![i197 class](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6033492/28076007-3fffecb8-662b-11e7-9a2d-b1023d473068.png)
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body: Subsumed by #208
Comments.Author: prmr

MDU6SXNzdWU5MTYxNTQ0Mw==.Title: State: copy and paste does not preserve edge labels
MDU6SXNzdWU5MTYxNTQ0Mw==.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU5MTYxNTQ0Mw==.Body: If you copy a group with a labeled edge, the edge label does not appear in the copied version.

Comments.Body: This got fixed as part of #106
Comments.Author: prmr

MDU6SXNzdWUzNTk5ODU0NDk=.Title: Remember Image format
MDU6SXNzdWUzNTk5ODU0NDk=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWUzNTk5ODU0NDk=.Body: For a given session, remember the last image format used and set it by default in the list of image formats. Also, set `png` as the default image format.

Comments.Body: The `graph` package is becoming quite complex and I need to pull back. With @EJBQ's help I'm reverting the management of edges inside node. From now on we'll just use the addNode and addEdge callbacks to deal with the children node management, so we can keep the `GraphElement` subinterfaces as small as possible.
Comments.Author: prmr

MDU6SXNzdWU1NTcxNTY0MzY=.Title: Extra diagram type suffix get added to file name
MDU6SXNzdWU1NTcxNTY0MzY=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU1NTcxNTY0MzY=.Body:
**Description**
When saving a file name, the diagram type suffix (e.g., `.class`) gets added to the name of the file even if it already exists in the name, resulting in a double of the type in the suffix, e.g., `.class.class.jet`.

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Create new class diagram 
2. Save As | "Test" -> the file gets saved as `test.class.jet` (ok)
3. Save As again. The name `test.class` appears in the file chooser. Select save -> The previous file is overwritten (ok)
4. Save As again. Remove the last character (`s`), then type it back again, so the name in the file choose is still `test.class`, as in step 3. Select Save -> The file gets saved as `test.class.class` (not ok)

**Configuration**
 - JetUML Release: 2.5
 - OS + version: Applies to all
 - Java version: Applies to all

**Additional Details**
The problem is actually caused by JavaFX's `FileChooser`, which does not by default recognize 2-level file extensions. It seems impossible to solve at the moment.

MDU6SXNzdWUxMTM4NTQwOTA=.Title: Package North connection point not always on figure outline
MDU6SXNzdWUxMTM4NTQwOTA=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWUxMTM4NTQwOTA=.Body: The connection point for the package node lies on its bounding box which, due to its shape, can sometimes appear to be floating in mid-air.

Comments.Body: This only sort of works now for `NoteEdge`s because the other types of edges don't call `getConnectionPoints` on the start and end nodes as part of their `draw` method.
Comments.Author: prmr

I_kwDOAblrlc5KatXg.Title: Add to Architectural Documentation
I_kwDOAblrlc5KatXg.Author: prmr
I_kwDOAblrlc5KatXg.Body: The design of the diagram rendering feature involves a number of non-trivial mechanisms that would benefit from additional documentation.

MDU6SXNzdWUxNTIwMzQ0NTU=.Title: Invisible toolbar buttons in small frame
MDU6SXNzdWUxNTIwMzQ0NTU=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWUxNTIwMzQ0NTU=.Body: When the `GraphFrame` has a small size, some of the lower toolbar buttons become inaccessible. The toolbar should be redesigned with a flow layout so that they expand horizontally to fill two columns and remain visible.

MDU6SXNzdWU3ODg0NTMwNQ==.Title: Use Case Diagram Cut Undo Problem for Groups
MDU6SXNzdWU3ODg0NTMwNQ==.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU3ODg0NTMwNQ==.Body: In a use case diagram, cutting a group of nodes and edges and undoing replaces only part of the group. It takes multiple additional undo commands to replace the entire group.

MDU6SXNzdWU0MDE1MjMxMjk=.Title: Click on OK button in properties dialog using 'Enter' key not working
MDU6SXNzdWU0MDE1MjMxMjk=.Author: stumash
MDU6SXNzdWU0MDE1MjMxMjk=.Body:

In the properties dialog, I can use the 'tab' key to move between the different text input boxes and the OK button at the bottom of the dialog. However, I cannot click the OK button simply by hitting the enter key. It would be nice to be able to do this, since it seems to be possible in some, but not all, of the dialog boxes in the application.

**Configuration**
 - JetUML Release: `2.3`
 - OS + version:
```
$ uname -a

Linux storktop 4.15.0-43-generic #46~16.04.1-Ubuntu SMP Fri Dec 7 13:31:08 UTC 2018 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```
 - Java version:
```
$ java -version

java version "1.8.0_191"
Java(TM) SE Runtime Environment (build 1.8.0_191-b12)
Java HotSpot(TM) 64-Bit Server VM (build 25.191-b12, mixed mode)
```

I_kwDOAblrlc52tw0q.Title: JavaFX GUI Unit Testing
I_kwDOAblrlc52tw0q.Author: ArthusWQZ
I_kwDOAblrlc52tw0q.Body:
GUI unit tests using JavaFX components should be carefully handled.

In fact, JavaFX uses a **single-threaded** rendering design, which means every GUI operations must be done in the **JavaFX Application Thread**. This is important since **JUnit tests are _not_ executed in the JavaFX Application Thread**.

### What does that mean?
 
When designing GUI-related unit tests, GUI operations must be executed explicitly in the JavaFX Application Thread, using `Platform.runLater(Runnable pRunnable)`. However, JUnit assertions **must not** be executed in the JavaFX Application Thread.

Therefore, it is good practice to only use `Platform.runLater()` for GUI calls, and fit the rest of the logic in the test method, as one would usually do.

**IMPORTANT NOTE:** `Platform.runLater()` queues the Runnable in the event queue. This queue is responsible for what the Application Thread runs. Then, the code passed in the Runnable **may _not_ be run instantly**. However, there is a workaround by calling this method right after the `Platform.runLater()` call:

```java
public static void waitForRunLater() throws InterruptedException
    {
        Semaphore semaphore = new Semaphore(0);
        Platform.runLater(() -> semaphore.release());
        semaphore.acquire();
    }
```
The `semaphore.release()` call will be following the Runnable in the event queue, then the execution of the test method will resume as soon as the Runnable has been run. This is crucial since omitting this detail can result in the test ending before the first Runnable was even run.

### JavaFX Platform Initialization Issue

Before performing any `Platform.runLater()` call, the Platform itself should be initialized (otherwise the call will result in an `IllegalStateException: Toolkit not initialized`).

This can be done by two different ways:
- Calling `Platform.startup(Runnable pRunnable)` first. Then using `Platform.runLater()` only. While this is ok for running a single test class, it can be a problem when running several test classes in a row, since `Platform.start(Runnable pRunnable)` **can only be run once** (otherwise results in an `IllegalStateException: Toolkit already initialized`).
- Using the provided [`JavaFXLoader`](https://github.com/prmr/JetUML/blob/master/test/org/jetuml/JavaFXLoader.java) class by calling `JavaFXLoader.load()` once before running any test (this can be done in a static void method with the `@BeforeAll` JUnit annotation). This way is better than the other since it works whether a single test class is run or multiple ones.

### JavaFX Platform Exiting Requirements

It is necessary to ensure that every GUI component is closed before the test run terminates. For instance, `Stage` objects can be closed with `Stage#close()`. Since the Platform implicitly exits at the end of the test run, an error can be print out to the console if some GUI components is not closed yet. This error does not seem to affect the quality of the test run, but it is good practice to avoid it.

Once again, GUI components can be closed after all tests have been run using a static void method with the `@AfterAll` JUnit annotation.

### Possible solution to explore

Using the JUnit extension system, it would be possible to initialize the Platform before running the first test, and to explicitly close it (hence there would be no need to close every GUI component, since it would act as a _force_ close) once all the tests from all test files have been run. 

Then, every GUI-related test class would just need the `@ExtendWith({Extension.class})` JUnit annotation to register as a JavaFX-dependent class requiring the JavaFX toolkit.

Comments.Body: Partially implemented as part of #508
Comments.Author: prmr

MDU6SXNzdWUzMDg3ODkxMDE=.Title: Add Scrollbars in JavaFX UI
MDU6SXNzdWUzMDg3ODkxMDE=.Author: kkutschera
MDU6SXNzdWUzMDg3ODkxMDE=.Body:
In pull request #237, attempts were made to add scrollbars. In the last commit, https://github.com/prmr/JetUML/pull/237/commits/e41a80f55ea37b209d147cc24e280c0682c0693b, the following issues were still unresolved:
1) `ScrollPane` Viewing Area when `Canvas` is Resized
The `ScrollPane` viewing area is controlled by the scrollbar’s `hValue` and `vValue` properties. These properties are numbers between 0 and 1 that control the position of the scrollbars (for example, `hValue==1` means the rightmost position). Because of this, if `hValue==1` then the viewing area follows the rightmost edge of the canvas, otherwise the viewing area looks at whichever area is corresponding to the current `hValue` and `vValue` properties. This can be awkward, for example, if you set the scrollbar’s values to 1 and drag a node to the bottom-right corner of the viewing area, the viewing area follows, but if you take another node and drag it up to the top-left corner the viewing area, it doesn’t follow. From further research there doesn't seem to be a reliable way to get the viewport location and size of the `ScrollPane` since the provided `viewportBounds` property is not reliably updated when the viewport changes (it appears to only update if the window is resized and not if the scrollbars are moved)
2) Node Jumping to Meet Mouse
When dragging a node that is responsible for determining the graph bounds into the original viewing area of the `ScrollPane`, when the scrollbars disappear the node will jump to meet the position of the mouse (that is if the scrollbars are following the node’s position).
3) Responsiveness/Lag
There is more lag than with the Swing UI, which is believed to be due to canvas size.  The `Canvas` has to be created with a maximum size otherwise it does not appear at all.

Additionally, note that in https://github.com/prmr/JetUML/pull/237/commits/fec8554dbbbf6465cd4755f88f86f72d7708456b logic was added so that when the user drags the node past the canvas and drags it back there is no disconnect between the mouse location and the node location on the canvas.

Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body: I don't foresee any problems with that plan. Since the scrollbars are based on the size of the window, you shouldn't get any viewport issues like we had when trying to dynamically change the canvas size.
Comments.Author: kkutschera

Comments.Body:
There is till work left to do on this one, namely:
* Automatically pan the scrollpane viewport as a selection is dragged outside the visible boundaries;
* Allow the user to set the canvas size.

However, these improvements should be open as new issues, since they will be much easier to implement after #257 is done.
Comments.Author: prmr

MDU6SXNzdWU2MzAyNzQyNDI=.Title: Auto-activate the Selection Tool
MDU6SXNzdWU2MzAyNzQyNDI=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU2MzAyNzQyNDI=.Body:
A common usage scenario is to create a number of diagram elements, then to select them. However, this requires first activating the selection tool, and it's easy for the user to forget this, and end up creating a bunch of unwanted elements instead of selecting one. 

To avoid this issue, the following behavior can be implemented:

**If** the user clicks on the canvas **and** the click is within an object's bound, **then** automatically activate the Selection Tool in the toolbar and select the element clicked.

A special case needs to be added to handled the addition of child nodes of `PackageNode`, however, because in this case we do want to add a node.

MDU6SXNzdWU3Mzc0NzE0OQ==.Author: prmr

2. Select the entire diagram
3. Move the entire selection right-left or down. The call nodes move down.

Additionally, if a "created" ImplicitParameterNode is part of the selection, it is not moved.

Comments.Body:
Quick fix for solving the created nodes not moving:
- in `GraphPanelMouseMotionListener.mouseDragged` change the line:

`if(!aSelectedElements.contains(n.getParent())`

to

`if(!aSelectedElements.contains(n.getParent()) || n instanceof ImplicitParameterNode) // parents are responsible for translating their children
`

So created nodes don't get skipped,
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body: Hi, thanks for reporting this bug. Could you please confirm that after clicking on the menu item, you do not drag the mouse below the menu item. Because it's standard JavaFX behavior to clear the menu if you click-then-drag. To pin the menu, you have to just click.

Actually I have tried both with dragging and without dragging my mouse but I got the same result.

Below is some results that you may find helpful..

   => *Drop-down menu disappear*

   => *Drop-down menu disappear and No command executed*
 
 - Right click on `File`, drag mouse arrow to any drop-down menu option and Left click

 - Used `Alt`+`F` and then without releasing any key, used `up` and `down` key to select any option from drop-down menu and hit `Enter` key
   => *Drop-down menu disappear and Selected command executed* ✔️

MDU6SXNzdWU2ODYzMjY2MzQ=.Title: Make connections/edges smarter
MDU6SXNzdWU2ODYzMjY2MzQ=.Author: johannesrave
MDU6SXNzdWU2ODYzMjY2MzQ=.Body:
**Description**
When more than a few edges are on screen, they interfere with each other and make the diagram hard to read graphically. I spend considerable time repositioning the nodes, just to avoid this. When deciding on an overall layout of a diagram, I think the edge obstructions shouldn't be this much of a concern.

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Draw a diagram with lots of inheritance or composition from more than one source node.
2. Look at it.

**Screenshots**
![grafik](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/31467653/91308965-53897880-e7b0-11ea-96f8-2894a2355115.png)


**Configuration**
 - JetUML Release: 3.0
 - OS + version: Windows 10
 - Java version openjdk version "13.0.2"
 
**Additional information**
I am taking my first steps toward learning JavaFX in my CS degree, so maybe I could tackle this. I haven't yet contributed to OSS yet though, so would need an introduction, but I am looking for a project to contribute to.

Comments.Body: Related to #230. However, the latter is the refactoring required to enable the edge layout improvement. There is definitely a plan to improve the edge layout, but making this layout editable is out of scope as it takes away from the minimalist vision for the tool.
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body: I absolutely see your point about the scope of JetUML, and expected this perspective. It'd be nice to still enable my usecase of having medium-sized diagrams with a certain amount of edges with robust automatic placement, so I'm glad work in this direction is planned.
Comments.Author: johannesrave

Comments.Body:
## Design Sketch:
1. Provide an _edge category_ scheme in terms of their visual appearance. For example, implementation edges, extension edges, composition edges, etc.
2. Establish a _priority_ scheme so that edges in one category get drawn first (so that it's possible to take their layout into account when drawing other edges). For examples, extension edges go first, then implementation edges, etc.
3. Establish a fix scheme for attaching edges to nodes based on their category. For example, extension edges always attach to the center of a node's side, then immediately after come the implementation edges, etc.
4. The edge's attachment to the node should take into account its position relative to the node, so we avoid edge crossings.
5. This will require to move away from the pure-stateless edge drawing algorithm, since edge layouts will need to be remembered between categories. We will this have a system based on passes through the edges. 

## Visual Examples

In this example the position 0 is used by the extends edge and the position (1) on each side is used by implementation edges.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6033492/106152732-ca55e380-614b-11eb-851f-ff147657d4b7.png)

In this example the current layout has the extends edge group slightly to the left. In the new design this edge group would reclaim position 0, and the second priority edge (implements) would be the one shifted to position 1. Additionally, the edge can be drawn to avoid intersecting with the higher-priority edge.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6033492/106153823-f4f46c00-614c-11eb-935b-5876c261692e.png)
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body:

I_kwDOAblrlc5eq_G4.Title: Flashing back and forth children nodes in PackageNode
I_kwDOAblrlc5eq_G4.Author: RuoyuDeng
I_kwDOAblrlc5eq_G4.Body: **Description** When one creates a class diagram with a package node that contains at least three children nodes, this bug will show up. The width of the package node is wider than what can be displayed on canvas. (e.g, the display zone has width 1000, and the package node has width 2000). The full content in the package can not be seen without scrolling the slide bar on the bottom. Let there be class node C1,C2,C3 in the package node. C1,C3 are located at the beginning of the left and right side of the package node and C1,C3 can not be displayed at the same time. After setting up such case, when one tries to move C2, it starts flying all around the places. **Steps to reproduce** 1. Set diagram size to Width = 2000, Height = 1000 2. Create new 'class' diagram 3. Click on 'package' 4. Create 3 'class' nodes inside of the package 5. Move one of them to the left boundary of the canvas and one of them to the right boundary of the canvas 6. Any trial to move the third class node should cause the error to occur **Screenshots** Before Zoom Out: After Zoom Out: **Configuration** - JetUML Release: Integration 3.6 - OS + version: macOS Ventura 13.1 - Java version: 18.0.2.1 - JavaFx version: 19 **Additional information** Zoom out can resolve such a problem because if we can see the left and right boundary of the package node at the same time, the renderer can make right decision about what to do when we move nodes around the place.

Comments.Body:
This issue occurs when trying to auto-scroll while dragging with a selection that exceeds the size of the viewport (area visible in a diagram tab). The auto-scroll feature attempts to always show the entire selection in the direction of the drag (e.g., dragging right, the right parts of the bounds). The calculations are correct but the user experience is not great.

To mitigate the issue, this fix implements two changes:
1. The bounds of parent nodes are not automatically included in the bounds of a selection, which makes it possible to move the nodes within a package (for example) without the viewport trying to show the entire package. In small viewport environments, the UX will be greatly improved.
2. When the bounds of the selection exceed the viewport in a dimension (X or Y), auto-scrolling automatically stops to prevent the jitter. Users can still scroll manually to adjust their viewport.
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body:
Any movement on this?

It seems the problem is caused by the last `for` loop in `Clipboard.pasteInto(GraphPanel pGraphPanel, boolean pOriginalPositions)`. This makes a call to `Graph.connect`, and inside this call one call node is not found, and re-created. Besides the bug, this implementation appears to be inconsistent with the Javadoc of the method connect, which says that if both nodes are not found, the edge is not added. This needs to be inspected more carefully.

Comments.Body: The bug seems to be resolved on my end. I just added a small if statement to skip the Graph.connect step on CallNode->CallNode edges. This seems to do the trick, although it doesn't actually solve whatever the underlying problem is. I've also refactored the Clipboard class to comply with the Checkstyle guidelines. Everything should be good to go for Milestone 1.0 now.

MDU6SXNzdWUzMzA3MDQ1Nzc=.Title: Fix Spurious Scrollbars
MDU6SXNzdWUzMzA3MDQ1Nzc=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWUzMzA3MDQ1Nzc=.Body:
When the application window is resized so that the diagram is not entirely visible in one dimension (say, width), scrollbars will appear in both dimensions. 

This problem is the result of the conscious design decision to use `ScrollPane.setMaxSize(...)` in `DiagramTab`. Because the ScrollPane's size is bounded, the extra space occupied by the scrollbar will cause the viewport to shrink in the other dimension and the max size property, required for centering, will prevent allowing more space for it. 

The real issue here is that it turns out to be extremely non-obvious to find an elegant solution to center a non-resizable node (the `DiagramCanvas`) wrapped in a `ScrollPane` as the center node of a `BorderPane`.

The desired behavior is that a diagram canvas that is smaller than the scrollpane viewport would flow naturally to the center of the available tab pane, and become scrollable if the window is resized to a size smaller than the canvas.

Unfortunately, even if the node wrapped by the `ScrollPane` is non-resizable, the `ScrollPane` will still expand to fill the entire available space of the `BorderPane` center, which invariably positions the diagram canvas to the top left of the tab pane. 

The issue is not judged to be severe enough to pollute the code with hard-nosed solutions involving listeners or programmatic layouting. 

The desired solution should be limited to a clean layout configuration in the constructor of `DiagramTab`

MDU6SXNzdWU1NTA0NTczNTE=.Title: Make mode of package node mutually exclusive
MDU6SXNzdWU1NTA0NTczNTE=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU1NTA0NTczNTE=.Body:
Currently, package nodes can *both* have children *and* contain text, which is not well supported by the rendering because typically the drawing of the children nodes will hide the text. This eventuality should be prevented.

## Solution Alternatives

* Create two variant of PackageNode ("containers") and ("descriptors"), and have them as separate options in the toolbar. Would break backward compatibility. **Solution selected**.

* Use input validation to prevent the setting of text in a node currently containing children, or vice-versa. This is by far the least invasive solution, but it would be able to circumvent it by modifying the persisted version of the diagram (acceptable).

MDU6SXNzdWU3MjI1NzE3MQ==.Title: Extra call node creation
MDU6SXNzdWU3MjI1NzE3MQ==.Author: EJBQ
MDU6SXNzdWU3MjI1NzE3MQ==.Body:
This is again with sequence diagrams.

Steps to reproduce:
Create two implicit parameter nodes
Give them one call child each
Connect the call nodes
Delete the target call node
Undo the deletion

What this does, which you can check by giving the edge and node non-default properties, is create a new call node at the end of the same edge when it is put back. Then the original call node is placed below the new one. Both new call nodes are children of the original parent call node.

If you delete the commits one by one going into the past, I'm seeing this issue appear with the merge of edge lists in nodes.

Comments.Body: Ah, I get it. Is the callee still a child node? I thought we had refactored this away.
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body:
The callee is still a listed as part of aCalls, which is the separate storage that calls have for their children now.

So what I'm seeing with this is the edge is never added to originEdges or terminalEdges for either callNode. Then when the edge is deleted, because it is not in those lists, when removeEdge is called on the nodes, they return true, which breaks the for loop of notifying the edges.. I'm not sure in what cases that method is supposed to return true or false though.

I just pushed a change that I think fixes it, but there definitely still issues with the origin and terminal edge lists that I pointed to above. I have tested this change thoroughly yet, but it seems to work for me.
Comments.Author: EJBQ

MDU6SXNzdWUxMDM3NzM0Mzg=.Title: Overlapping Edges in Diagrams
MDU6SXNzdWUxMDM3NzM0Mzg=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWUxMDM3NzM0Mzg=.Body:
In all diagrams except sequence diagrams, adding multiple edges between two nodes invisibly stacks the edges. For class, object, state, and use case diagrams, this should not be possible. For sequence diagrams, the edge is re-drawn lower and lower: this should be fixed.
At the same time, the arrow end properties should be editable to make it possible to have bidirectional edges. We could even consider adding a bidirectional edge prototype to the toolbar.

Comments.Body: There is quite a bit of work to do to improve the edges management, including creating specialized classes for aggregation, dependency, etc.
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body: With the new design for the edge collection we can simply constrain diagrams (except sequence diagrams) to have a single type of edge between two nodes (one per direction).
Comments.Author: prmr

I_kwDOAblrlc5zHY1y.Title: Notification-based Feedback Mechanism
I_kwDOAblrlc5zHY1y.Author: ArthusWQZ
I_kwDOAblrlc5zHY1y.Body:
Implement a non-obtrusive notification-based feedback mechanism using toast notifications.

**GUI part**

- [x] Implement the Notification interface
- [x] Implement the notification handling logic in the EditorFrame class
- [x] Implement the ToastNotification class
- [x] Add a dialog to allow user to set notification time, or disable it
- [x] Add a tip to describe this feature

**Functional part**

Identify unvalidated semantic constraint to display a notification to the user with an explanation as to why the semantic constraint was broken.

MDU6SXNzdWU5NzU3Mzc2NTA=.Title: Cannot select overlapped implicit parameters in sequence diagram
MDU6SXNzdWU5NzU3Mzc2NTA=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU5NzU3Mzc2NTA=.Body:
**Description**
In a sequence diagram, if an implicit parameter is position above another one (because of constructor call layouts), one of the two cannot be selected, depending on which one is selected.

**Steps to reproduce**

1. Create new sequence diagram as show below 
2. Make sure one implicit parameter node is position above the other
3. Try to select the node that's not selected.
4. It does not select.

**Screenshots**
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6033492/130261208-1454d9bf-f193-4def-9df6-0c2dbd2e20d7.png)

**Configuration**
 - JetUML Release: 3.3
 - OS + version: All
 
**Workaround**

Reposition the nodes to enable the selection, then reposition as desired.

I_kwDOAblrlc5CFacE.Title: Make it possible to reoder tabs in the diagram editor
I_kwDOAblrlc5CFacE.Author: prmr

MDU6SXNzdWU2MjY5MTMxOTQ=.Title: Call Node Mispositioned in the Sequence Diagram
MDU6SXNzdWU2MjY5MTMxOTQ=.Author: cc-21
MDU6SXNzdWU2MjY5MTMxOTQ=.Body: **Description** When clicking on the first `CallNode` in the constructor call, the `CallNode` will be reordered and thus placed in the wrong position. The position of the `CallNode` should not be changed when clicked. **Steps to reproduce** 1. Create a new sequence diagram 2. Create the diagram as attached in **Screenshots** 3. Click the first `CallNode` 4. See error **Screenshots** **Configuration** - JetUML Release: 2.6 - OS + version: All - Java version: All

MDU6SXNzdWU2OTMxOTU0OQ==.Title: Polish the ToolBar Redesign
MDU6SXNzdWU2OTMxOTU0OQ==.Author: JoelChev
MDU6SXNzdWU2OTMxOTU0OQ==.Body: Implement a new ToolBar design that takes up less space and is more pleasing to the eye.

Comments.Body: I pushed it to master already (I will be making a branch for this issue shortly), but I have added a keyboard shortcut for selecting elements. The shortcut is CTRL-G (G for Grabber). This addition is currently in master, I encourage you guys to check it out, it should be useful for quickly making diagrams.

Comments.Body: Following your recommendations @prmr I have improved the look and feel of toolbars. I'm going to close the issue now and merge this branch into master.

I_kwDOAblrlc5PSSSH.Title: Implement Stateful Pass-based Sequence Diagram Rendering
I_kwDOAblrlc5PSSSH.Author: prmr
I_kwDOAblrlc5PSSSH.Body: The current implementation is purely stateless, which less to a complex and inefficient design. With the recent refactoring (#466) it should be possible to implement the rendering of the sequence diagram in a single pass, a bit like the class diagram. This will also contribute to making the `SequenceDiagramBuilder` simpler, and help solve bug #463.

MDU6SXNzdWU4NzEzMDM1Mzc=.Title: Font Size Feature
MDU6SXNzdWU4NzEzMDM1Mzc=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU4NzEzMDM1Mzc=.Body: It should be possible to change the size of the font used when rendering diagrams.

MDU6SXNzdWUxMTg4NDQ1MjA=.Title: Editable Final Property on Circular States Can Break Diagram Semantics
MDU6SXNzdWUxMTg4NDQ1MjA=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWUxMTg4NDQ1MjA=.Body: There is a rule preventing edges into initial states and out of final states, but since it's possible to edit the `final` property of the state, these constraints can be violated. The property should not be user-editable.

MDU6SXNzdWUxMDM2MDM5NTU=.Title: Support undirected association in class diagrams
MDU6SXNzdWUxMDM2MDM5NTU=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWUxMDM2MDM5NTU=.Body: Add an edge type with no arrowhead.

MDU6SXNzdWU1Mzk2NzI3Ng==.Title: Add a diagram type fragment to file extensions
MDU6SXNzdWU1Mzk2NzI3Ng==.Author: prmr

Comments.Body:
This issue used to be titled "Move to the Violet >0.16 serialization format" but after a bit of research it seems like the 0.16 and 0.21 formats are the same (JavaBean-enabled), the only difference being that in 0.21 the files get an additional extension fragment to indicate the diagram type. 

However, one of the big drawbacks of the JB serialization framework is that simply renaming the packages breaks backward compatibility. Writing a command-line converter should be simple enough though. See Issue #17.
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body: @prmr I'm not sure if this helps with the problem at all, but I found a field marked "files.extension" in the UMLEditorStrings.properties file in the com.horstmann.violet package. Would modifying this field to a different extension do anything to fix this issue?
Comments.Author: JoelChev

Comments.Body: Worth a try indeed.
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body: @prmr Ok. I will look at it. You just want a diagram type fragment added in front of the .violet extension depending on the type of diagram being saved right? So a Class diagram should now be saved as blahblah.class.violet for instance?
Comments.Author: JoelChev

Comments.Body: Yes, correct.
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body: I believe I have succeeded in adding a diagram type file extension to saved files. Please check out branch extended_file_extension and play around with saving & loading files. I haven't written any tests, but it seems to work on my end. I haven't tested it without the serialization removed (which @prmr did recently) though, so testing a later merged branch could also be useful.
Comments.Author: JoelChev

Comments.Body: There's a piece missing for managed file type descriptions in the file chooser. It does not regognize specific extension types. Have a look at how 0.21 does it. It recognizes specific diagram types, and also a generic "All violet files" for anything ending in .violet
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body: I need to change `EditorFrame` as part of issue #26 so I'll wait until you integrate your brand into master before starting with this. BTW don't forget to name your branch according to the naming convention.
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body: @prmr I see what you mean. I will look at it today and tomorrow and try to put that feature in. Thanks for pointing it out!
Comments.Author: JoelChev

Comments.Body:
Ok, I just added the features that you outlined above @prmr. I didn't implement any tests for this, but when I played around with saving and opening files, the new diagram-specific extensions seemed to be behaving as they would in 0.21. If you guys can please check out this branch and test it for yourself, I would appreciate it. 

An important note however is that by changing the FileService class in this recent commit, I started to see errors in the JNLPFileService class. @EJBQ  this appears to be a class involved with Java Web Start, so as you are removing this the error shouldn't be an issue for our purposes. If it is please let me know and I can look into correcting the code in JNLPFileServices too.
Comments.Author: JoelChev

Comments.Body: @JoelChev The class is deleted, so those shouldn't come up. It's gone in master as well now.
Comments.Author: EJBQ

MDU6SXNzdWUzNzU5ODkzNzM=.Title: Call edges inserted in wrong order in sequence diagram
MDU6SXNzdWUzNzU5ODkzNzM=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWUzNzU5ODkzNzM=.Body:
**Description**
Adding a call edg m2 below another call edge m1 positions m2 above m1 in the call sequence

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Create a sequence diagram with 3 objects A, B, C
2. Add a call edge m1 between A and B.
3. Add a call edge m2 between A and C by pointing the mouse below m1
The call is placed above m1

**Screenshots**
![sequence](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6033492/47794790-8c14b400-dcf7-11e8-8bc1-5fe9fb8fa4cf.png)

**Configuration**
 - JetUML Release: 2.2
 - OS + version: Windows 10
 - Java version: 10.0.2

MDU6SXNzdWUzMjIzOTMyNzQ=.Title: Monitor selection in dual-screen setup
MDU6SXNzdWUzMjIzOTMyNzQ=.Author: nassifm
MDU6SXNzdWUzMjIzOTMyNzQ=.Body: When using JetUML on a dual-screen setup, with the main window being on the second monitor, all dialogs and right-click menu appears in the first monitor, instead of where they should.

Comments.Body:
For the popup menu, the issue is caused by a mismatch between scene and screen coordinates. 

Method `DiagramFrameToolBar.showPopup` takes in a point argument supplied in scene coordinates and passes it to `ContextMenu,show` which expects a point in screen coordinates. Same with the property sheet.

For the properties sheet, the issue is a lack of API documentation.

It seems (?) that by default creating a new `Stage` will center it on the primary screen. The documentation of the `Stage.centerOnScreen()` seems to be inaccurate because inserting a call to it when creating the property sheet still does not position it on the "current" screen, whatever that is.
Comments.Author: prmr

I_kwDOAblrlc5p1-qb.Title: Node order not preserved during serialization
I_kwDOAblrlc5p1-qb.Author: prmr
I_kwDOAblrlc5p1-qb.Body:
**Description**
The order of nodes is not maintained when a diagram is encoded into JSON. This can lead to unpredictable results when re-loading the diagram, because some aspects of the rendering rely on node ordering (in particular to deal with overlapping nodes). 

**Steps to reproduce**
(_non-deterministic_)
1. Create a new class diagram (for example)
2. Create three class nodes, then three note nodes.
3. Position the note nodes directly on top of the class nodes
4. Save the file and close the diagram.
5. Load the file: some note nodes may now be underneath the class nodes.

**Configuration**
 - JetUML Release: 3.5
 
**Additional information**
The reliance on the map data structure in the `JsonEncoder` causes this problem.

MDU6SXNzdWUxNDc4MTIyODI=.Title: Copy to Clipboard no longer clips the graph bounds
MDU6SXNzdWUxNDc4MTIyODI=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWUxNDc4MTIyODI=.Body:
To reproduce:
1. Create a diagram with a class node
2. Copy to clipboard
3. Paste into a drawing program.

Everything is fine.
1. Move the node far to the right
2. Move the node back left to close to the origin
3. Paste into a drawing program
   The bounds the the drawing are to the furthest point the node was moved.

I_kwDOAblrlc5rH7vX.Title: Package node loses position when last child node unlinked
I_kwDOAblrlc5rH7vX.Author: prmr
I_kwDOAblrlc5rH7vX.Body:
**Description**
The position of package nodes is a function of the position of their children nodes. However, when all nodes are deleted, the package node should retain its last position. This occurs correctly when children nodes are _deleted_, but not when they are _unlinked_ from the parent node.

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Create new class diagram 
2. Create a new package node
3. Add some children nodes to the package node
4. Move the children around
5. Unlink all the children from the package node
6. The package node jumps to the position where it was initially created

**Configuration**
 - JetUML Release: 3.5

MDU6SXNzdWUzNDcyMTc4MTQ=.Title: Add support for adding constructor calls in sequence diagram
MDU6SXNzdWUzNDcyMTc4MTQ=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWUzNDcyMTc4MTQ=.Body:
Rebuild the feature that was removed as part of the #264 refactoring. Create a new type of edge for constructor calls.

This feature is currently too difficult to implement given the limited support for analyzing sequence diagrams. It will have to follow a refactoring that allows better navigation between nodes and edges.

```
	at javafx.graphics/com.sun.javafx.tk.RenderJob.run(RenderJob.java:58)
	at java.base/java.util.concurrent.ThreadPoolExecutor.runWorker(ThreadPoolExecutor.java:1130)
	at java.base/java.util.concurrent.ThreadPoolExecutor$Worker.run(ThreadPoolExecutor.java:630)
	at javafx.graphics/com.sun.javafx.tk.quantum.QuantumRenderer$PipelineRunnable.run(QuantumRenderer.java:125)
	at java.base/java.lang.Thread.run(Thread.java:832)
```

MDU6SXNzdWU0MzAwMzY1OTY=.Title: Detect Version Mismatches
MDU6SXNzdWU0MzAwMzY1OTY=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU0MzAwMzY1OTY=.Body:
Diagrams loaded from a version that is not the current version should be automatically updated to the newest version, including transformed if necessary. The user should be informed of this so they are given an opportunity to save the file with a different name if they want.

Note that an alert is only shown if the diagram was actually transformed for the migration (as opposed to just have its version information updated). This is because if the diagram is not transformed, it remains compatible with previous versions of JetUML.

MDU6SXNzdWU1NTMzNTQ0Nw==.Title: Remove old applet-supporting code
MDU6SXNzdWU1NTMzNTQ0Nw==.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU1NTMzNTQ0Nw==.Body:
Some of the code and design complexity (e.g., the `FileService` class hierarchy) is only there to support applet mode, which we will not be supporting. This code should be removed and the result refactored to simplify.

I will also cleanup the business with the file extensions.

Comments.Body: Basically a move-up refactoring for `FileService` and `PersistenceService`
Comments.Author: prmr

MDU6SXNzdWUxODYxMzEzMzU=.Author: prmr

MDU6SXNzdWU2OTIzMjc5MjE=.Title: Impossible to add Field nodes to object nodes in Object diagrams
MDU6SXNzdWU2OTIzMjc5MjE=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU2OTIzMjc5MjE=.Body:
**Description**
Trying to add a field node to an object node results in the object node being selected instead.

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Create new Object diagram 
2. Create a new object node
3. Select field node creation tool in the toolbar to activate it
4. Click on the object node to add the field node.

The object node is selected instead.

**Configuration**
 - JetUML Release: 3.0
 - OS + version: All versions:
 - Java version: All versions
 
**Additional information**
This is a regression from #363 . The problems is that in `DiagramCanvasController.isNodeOtherThanPackageAndField` the code checks the destination node, not the node type to be created. Instead of `FieldNode`, it should be `ObjectNode` that is part of the condition.

```
private boolean isNodeOtherThanPackageAndField(DiagramElement pElement)
{
	return pElement instanceof Node && !(pElement instanceof PackageNode)&& !(pElement instanceof FieldNode);
}
```

I_kwDOAblrlc5qQo0D.Title: Correct middle label placement for straight segmented edges
I_kwDOAblrlc5qQo0D.Author: prmr
I_kwDOAblrlc5qQo0D.Body:
**Description**

Label placement calculations for segmented edges is not trivial. To look good, the middle label's position needs to depend on the geometry of the edge. 
![image](https://github.com/prmr/JetUML/assets/6033492/e3c39392-bc52-4aab-b4a4-34fcd6c2e16b)

The label is centered on the middle segment if horizontal, and to the right and slightly offset from the middle if vertical.

However, when the diagram geometry results in a straight edge, this formula makes the label appear off-center

![image](https://github.com/prmr/JetUML/assets/6033492/0360864f-b78a-41da-b8be-6f8f6eac46be)

We need to detect when edges are straight (no middle segment) and position the edge intuitively in such cases.



**Configuration**
 - JetUML Release: 3.5

MDU6SXNzdWUyODc0NzI5NTY=.Title: Middle Label label
MDU6SXNzdWUyODc0NzI5NTY=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWUyODc0NzI5NTY=.Body: The middle label property should have a more appropriate name. Probably the property name was switched and is no longer found in the resource file.

MDU6SXNzdWUyNzYyMzE4Njg=.Title: Remove icon generation responsibility from Toolbar
MDU6SXNzdWUyNzYyMzE4Njg=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWUyNzYyMzE4Njg=.Body: The `ToolBar` creates icons that require a call to `draw` every time the panel is repainted. Instead, refactor the code so that it generates images that can be embedded directly into buttons, which would eliminate a large number of invocations of `draw` methods on nodes and edges. At the same time, move the functionality to a separate class (`IconCreator`).

**Steps to reproduce**

2. Select All and Copy
3. Create a new, empty sequence diagram.
4. Paste into it.

The call edges don't have the proper gap. See screenshot 2.

![test2](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6033492/48324238-e6aafa00-e67f-11e8-92e0-e9fa42f7d732.png)

**Configuration**
 - JetUML Release: 2.2
 - OS + version: Windows 10
 - Java version: 10.0.2
 
**Additional information**
\[Replace this text with any other information about the problem here. Remove this section if not applicable.\]

Comments.Body: The pernicious requirement that `CallNodeView` objects require a reference `aDiagram` breaks the general copy-pasting mechanism across diagrams, since the target diagram is not set in the node by default.
Comments.Author: prmr

MDU6SXNzdWUyODUxNDA5Nzc=.Title: Refactor Reflection out of Properties
MDU6SXNzdWUyODUxNDA5Nzc=.Author: prmr

MDU6SXNzdWU2Mjg0NTY4NjU=.Title: Make AssociationEdge implicitly directed
MDU6SXNzdWU2Mjg0NTY4NjU=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU2Mjg0NTY4NjU=.Body:
Association edges should only be either in one direction, or bidirectional. It should not be possible to flip their directionality once created (for this one can create a new edge).

For consistency, it will be necessary to rename the enums of the directionality property: `{None, End, Both}` to `{Unspecified, Unidirectional, Bidirectional}`.

MDU6SXNzdWUxMTQ0ODg5MDA=.Title: Unused properties can be set on the return edge
MDU6SXNzdWUxMTQ0ODg5MDA=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWUxMTQ0ODg5MDA=.Body: The property editor for the `ReturnEdge` shows the unused properties. Either add a `BeanInfo` class or split the class off from the `SegmentedEdge` hierarchy.

MDU6SXNzdWU3MDM4MDUxNjI=.Title: Overagressive diagram version migration
MDU6SXNzdWU3MDM4MDUxNjI=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU3MDM4MDUxNjI=.Body:
**Description**
The version migrator attempts to migrate between compatible version, resulting in potential loss of information and a confusing message.

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Using a recent version (e.g., 3.0.1) save a class diagram with a dependency edge.
2. Open the saved file with a text editor and change the version number to 3.0
3. Reopen the file.
4. The version migrator is triggered.

**Configuration**
 - JetUML Release: 3.0.1
  
**Additional information**
The version migrator should only be activated when the version difference calls for a migration.

MDU6SXNzdWU1ODk3MTYxMg==.Title: Resolve bugs with Sequence Diagrams in Copy/Cut+Paste
MDU6SXNzdWU1ODk3MTYxMg==.Author: JoelChev
MDU6SXNzdWU1ODk3MTYxMg==.Body:
This will likely be encompassed in the larger re-design of the Diagrams and the Parent-Child relationships of their Nodes that we will be tackling later on, but for now I just want to make sure that the issue is not forgotten.

Steps to reproduce:
1. Create a new sequence diagram with a Call Edge between two ImplicitParameterNodes. 
2. Copy/Cut it.
3. Paste it.

The Call Nodes behave erratically and don't seem to be fixed properly to their lifelines. Again, this is something that will likely be addressed in a larger redesign, but I just want to make sure it is addressed.

Relevant: https://github.com/prmr/JetUML/wiki/Parent-Child-Relation

Comments.Body: I can no longer reproduce this bug. Was this fixed?
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body: I'm still able to reproduce the bug following the steps above. In case it wasn't clear above, the diagram pastes properly, but the pasted diagram behaves erratically when you try to move it at all (edges and nodes don't seem to be properly fixed in place). I think a redesign of Sequence Diagrams and the relationships between nodes and edges there will likely resolve this issue.
Comments.Author: JoelChev

I_kwDOAblrlc58BfYJ.Author: prmr

MDU6SXNzdWU4MTM1MTg2NjM=.Author: prmr

MDU6SXNzdWU4MDIyNDE5MDI=.Title: Can not move classes into packages
MDU6SXNzdWU4MDIyNDE5MDI=.Author: NumberC
MDU6SXNzdWU4MDIyNDE5MDI=.Body:
**Description**
When I attempt to move an already existing class into a package, the class does not go into the package. Instead, the class just overlaps with the package.

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Create a class
2. Create a package
3. Attempt to drag the class into the package

**Screenshots**
![ClassOverlapping](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/47584014/107050960-dd932f80-6799-11eb-9383-8be3db6b211d.PNG)

**Configuration**
 - JetUML Release: 3.1
 - OS + version: Windows 10
 - Java version: 15.0.1

**Possible Solution**
I think this may be resolved by editing the `mouseReleased()` class in the `DiagramCanvasController` class. As of now, when you `DRAG_MOVE`, it only aligns the node to the grid, instead of adding the node as a child when applicable.

Comments.Body:
This feature is achieved through a different gesture (tapping the <kbd>shift</kbd> key). Please see the [user guide](https://www.jetuml.org/docs/user-guide.html) or enable the "Tip of the day" from the help menu.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6033492/107052551-cce3b900-679b-11eb-8f1d-c2344f07ec69.png)

MDU6SXNzdWU2Mjc3MjU2ODQ=.Title: Capture interface stereotype as part of view, not data
MDU6SXNzdWU2Mjc3MjU2ODQ=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU2Mjc3MjU2ODQ=.Body:
For `InterfaceNode`s, the interface stereotype is stored in the diagram as part of the name, which is redundant. It is sufficient to show it only as part of the viewer.

This refactoring will require a migration of old diagrams to remove the redundant "interface" in the node name.

MDU6SXNzdWUzNjE4NzAzODc=.Title: Stagger pasted subgraphs
MDU6SXNzdWUzNjE4NzAzODc=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWUzNjE4NzAzODc=.Body: A subgraph added through pasting should be re-positioned to (x,x) if there is something at 0,0, and so on in GRID_SIZE pixel increments.

MDU6SXNzdWU3MTMxNzI1NDk=.Title: Unable to open diagram with large Canvas
MDU6SXNzdWU3MTMxNzI1NDk=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU3MTMxNzI1NDk=.Body:
**Description**
Depending on hardware, a large diagram size (set through `View | Set Diagram Size`) makes it look like nothing happens when a user attempts to create a new diagram or open any recent file that has too large a diagram size.

This problem is due to an [underlying JavaFX bug](https://bugs.openjdk.java.net/browse/JDK-8089835). The workaround is to use a smaller diagram size. Alternatively, [this Stack Overflow post](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/23467797/javafx-nullpointerexception-when-rendering-big-image) provides an alternative that uses command-line arguments to the virtual machine.

**Steps to reproduce**
It is only possible to reproduce this bug when JetUML is open on a display that cannot support the diagram size. 
1. Open JetUML and click `View | Set Diagram Size`, select the max size.
2. Create a new Class diagram (for example)

The following exception will be raised by the graphics system:

```
java.lang.NullPointerException
	at javafx.graphics/com.sun.javafx.sg.prism.NGCanvas$RenderBuf.validate(NGCanvas.java:213)
	at javafx.graphics/com.sun.javafx.sg.prism.NGCanvas.initCanvas(NGCanvas.java:641)
	at javafx.graphics/com.sun.javafx.sg.prism.NGCanvas.renderContent(NGCanvas.java:604)
	at javafx.graphics/com.sun.javafx.sg.prism.NGNode.doRender(NGNode.java:2072)
	at javafx.graphics/com.sun.javafx.sg.prism.NGNode.render(NGNode.java:1964)
	at javafx.graphics/com.sun.javafx.sg.prism.NGGroup.renderContent(NGGroup.java:270)
```

**Configuration**
 - JetUML Release: 2.1 and up
 - OS + version: Windows, Mac
 - Java version: 14

Comments.Body: This problem has not been reported in a while.
Comments.Author: prmr

MDU6SXNzdWUyMTU4ODc2MTM=.Title: Undo feature reverses call order after deletion in sequence diagram
MDU6SXNzdWUyMTU4ODc2MTM=.Author: aoldershaw
MDU6SXNzdWUyMTU4ODc2MTM=.Body:
# Description
"Undoing" the deletion of an activation bar with more than one call in a sequence diagram results in the order of the calls being reversed. The same result occurs upon the deletion of an entire implicit parameter with an activation bar containing more than one call.

# Steps to Reproduce
1. Open a new JetUML Sequence diagram
2. Create 2 implicit parameters, A and B, say. Create two calls from A to B, call1() and call2(), say, originating from the **same** activation bar. The diagram should look similar to this:
![diagram1](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/26583442/24172154/9036858c-0e5d-11e7-957d-525d05927e2a.png)
3. Select the activation bar on A, and delete it (Edit -> Delete)
4. Undo the deletion (Edit -> Undo). The diagram should now look similar to this:
![diagram2](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/26583442/24172210/c8d15cc8-0e5d-11e7-8410-45fbc9cad749.png)

MDU6SXNzdWU3NTc3NjU5MTk=.Title: Make static version of the user guide
MDU6SXNzdWU3NTc3NjU5MTk=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU3NTc3NjU5MTk=.Body: The user guide loads a bit slowly because it is built on-demand. Serving a generated static page will improve the user experience.

MDU6SXNzdWUxMTAyODc3MDQ=.Title: Cutting/deleting compound selection does not resize the package element
MDU6SXNzdWUxMTAyODc3MDQ=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWUxMTAyODc3MDQ=.Body:
To replicate:
0. Create class diagram with a package and two child class nodesl
0. Select a single class node and delete it. The package resizes. 
0. Undo to replace the class node
0. Select both class nodes with a lasso
0. Delete or cut.
The package element remains the same size. 
The problem is cause by removing all elements in the package, not by the fact that it's a compound selection.

MDU6SXNzdWUxMjU3MDIzMzQ=.Title: Node created on context menu creation tool selection
MDU6SXNzdWUxMjU3MDIzMzQ=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWUxMjU3MDIzMzQ=.Body: When using the context menu to select a node creation tool, selecting the tool creates a node at the same time, which is not the standard behavior expected from the menu. The tool should be selected, but no node should be created at the same time.

MDU6SXNzdWU0MzAwMzMwNzU=.Title: Simplify Dependencies in Class Diagrams
MDU6SXNzdWU0MzAwMzMwNzU=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU0MzAwMzMwNzU=.Body:
Dependencies should:
- [X] Not be allowed as self-edges
- [X] Offer support for bi-directionality
- [X] Not be individually settable between two given nodes, in both directions.
- [X] Have a single label
- [X] Use the default connection point


*This is a sub-task of #230. One issue with this refactoring is that adding a property (navigability) to dependency edges will make the file format backward-incompatible, which needs to be handled carefully.*

MDU6SXNzdWU5ODY3Mzc2NzU=.Title: Update Checkstyle style file to 8.44
MDU6SXNzdWU5ODY3Mzc2NzU=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU5ODY3Mzc2NzU=.Body:
Release 8.44 of Checkstyle has a backward-incompatible change:
```
<module name="JavadocMethod">
  <property name="scope" value="protected"/>
</module>
```
under the TreeWalker module, due to a breaking change in checkstyle 8.42 (8.44 is latest) this should now be
```
<module name="JavadocMethod">
  <property name="accessModifiers" value="public, protected"/>
</module>
```

MDU6SXNzdWU2NjY5OTIwMw==.Title: Bug with adding object edge to field node and undoing
MDU6SXNzdWU2NjY5OTIwMw==.Author: EJBQ

Comments.Body: Previously adding an edge to the new field node would take away the "value" string, and this was not tracked so it could not be undone. This is fixed now.
Comments.Author: EJBQ

MDU6SXNzdWU1NjU5MjIyNDI=.Title: Child nodes not staggered when copy-pasted
MDU6SXNzdWU1NjU5MjIyNDI=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU1NjU5MjIyNDI=.Body:
**Description**
When nodes are copied, they appear immediately below and to the right of the original node. This required behavior does not occur for nodes that are the child of another node (for example, class nodes nested within a package node).

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Create new class diagram
2. Create a new package node
3. Create a new class node within the package node
4. Select the package node, copy and paste it.
5. The copy appears staggered, which is desired.
6. Select the class node in either package node, copy it, paste it
7. The copy appears immediately on top of the original node, so it's not possible to see it unless it's moved.

**Configuration**
 - JetUML Release: 2.5+ (some later commit)
 - OS + version: All versions
 - Java version: All versions

Comments.Author: prmr

Fix copyrights
change the version tag
prepare release notes.

I_kwDOAblrlc4-5-G3.Title: Drag package content to bottom of viewport. Subsequence dragging misbehaves.
I_kwDOAblrlc4-5-G3.Author: frentos
I_kwDOAblrlc4-5-G3.Body: **Description** Drag a class nested in a package to the bottom of the viewport. Subsequent drag of any package content tracks mouse horizontally but not vertically. Expected behaviour: dragged content should track mouse movements. **Steps to reproduce** 1. Create new 'Class' diagram 2. Select "Package" tool 3. Click on canvas to create a package 4. Select "Class" tool 5. Click on canvas inside package boundary to create a class inside the package 6. Click on canvas inside package boundary to create a second class inside the package [now looks like fig1] 7. Select "Select" tool [ this step is optional; bug occurs if "Class" tool remains selected in the following steps ] 8. Click-and-drag one of the class objects [either will do] to the bottom of the viewport. Release mouse button. **Bottom edge of package is now slightly below the bottom edge of the viewport**. [attached fig2] 9. Click-and-drag the **other** class object. The selected class tracks left/right mouse movements, but creeps up so it is eventually no longer under the mouse cursor. Release the mouse button. [attached fig3] Attempts to retrieve the 'escaping' class object only make it move further up. 10. Click-and-drag the class object at the bottom of the screen. Like the other class, it tracks left-and-right but behaves as if fixed to the bottom of the viewport. **Screenshots** **Configuration** - JetUML Release: 3.3 - OS + version: macOS Mojave version 10.14.6 - Java version: Unknown. "java --show-version" reports "openjdk 11.0.12 2021-07-20" from a command prompt, but goodness knows what java version the app-launching apparatus drags in.

Comments.Body: Thanks for reporting. I can't reproduce the bottom edge part (step 8), but the rest is definitely evidence of a bug. We'll try to squeeze a fix into 3.4, the next release due by year end.
Comments.Author: prmr

MDU6SXNzdWUzMjEyMzE4NzQ=.Title: Remove dependencies to internal API types
MDU6SXNzdWUzMjEyMzE4NzQ=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWUzMjEyMzE4NzQ=.Body: In `SegmentedEdgeView` and `StateTransitionEdgeView`, remove dependencies to `com.sun.javafx...` types.

MDU6SXNzdWU1NzA1MTg5Mg==.Title: Misaligned return call in Sequence Diagram
MDU6SXNzdWU1NzA1MTg5Mg==.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU1NzA1MTg5Mg==.Body: When drawing a return edge, the arrow does not reach the original activation bar, it just floats in the air.

Comments.Body: I noticed this for a signal, which is normal behavior. This is a feature, not a bug
Comments.Author: prmr

MDU6SXNzdWU3ODUzMTc1NTE=.Title: Imperfect shape intersection for rounded rectangle and ellipse
MDU6SXNzdWU3ODUzMTc1NTE=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU3ODUzMTc1NTE=.Body:
**Description**
The current viewers do not compute a precise intersection between an edge and a shape that is not a rectangle or a circle. This includes rounded rectangles (states in state diagrams) and ellipses (use cases in use case diagrams).

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6033492/104490108-702b1f00-559e-11eb-862f-3b19f98357dc.png)

The reason for this is that up to now a simpler algorithm is used that computes the intersection with the shape's bounding box. For high-quality rendering of diagrams, it would be nice to compute the intersection precisely.

**Additional information**
The solution should respect the existing design, which is illustrated in the code through method `CircularStateNodeViewer#getConnectionPoint`. The intersection algorithms should be implemented in `GeomUtils` and tested, and then used in the node viewers for state and use case nodes, respectively.

I_kwDOAblrlc5N3j-G.Title: Edges don't connect to package node boundaries when connecting north.
I_kwDOAblrlc5N3j-G.Author: prmr
I_kwDOAblrlc5N3j-G.Body:
**Description**
The connection point for package nodes is computed using the node's bound. However, because the north side has a top rectangle, if the y-position is away from the top rectangle, it creates a gap between the connection point and the node's visible boundary.

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Create new class diagram 
2. Create two package nodes, as below.
3. Connect them with an edge
4. See error

**Screenshots**
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6033492/179297611-4ccaf300-d8eb-4bdd-ab70-e2d95585c8b2.png)

**Configuration**
 - JetUML Release: 3.4

MDU6SXNzdWUxNDMyNjU2Mzg=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWUxNDMyNjU2Mzg=.Body: The clone feature, available from the File menu and through a short-cut, should create a new diagram identical to the current diagram.

I_kwDOAblrlc5og0NT.Title: Not possible to create constructor call
I_kwDOAblrlc5og0NT.Author: prmr
I_kwDOAblrlc5og0NT.Body:
**Description**
Dragging between a call node and the top rectangle of an implicit parameter node creates a call instead of a constructor call.

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Create new sequence diagram 
2. Create two implicit parameter nodes
3. Drag from the life line of the first node to the top rectangle of the second node
4. A call edge is created instead of a constructor call edge

**Configuration**
 - JetUML Release: Integration branch `6e5f5c965fff64c62773b03f7283b8eb614ed2a7`

I_kwDOAblrlc5JEQWt.Title: Release 3.4
I_kwDOAblrlc5JEQWt.Author: prmr

I_kwDOAblrlc5pJeKn.Title: Additional semantic validation required for sequence diagrams
I_kwDOAblrlc5pJeKn.Author: prmr
I_kwDOAblrlc5pJeKn.Body:
**Description**
Additional semantic validation required for sequence diagrams. The rendering of sequence diagram is particularly sensitive to the semantic correctness of the diagram. Additional validations rules are necessary to prevent triggering exceptions in the rendering machinery for invalid diagrams. Doing this systematically will require a refactoring of the validation system.

* [x] Call Edge from call node to Note node
* [x] Call Edge from Note node to Note node, including self-node
* [x] Call Edge from top of implicit parameter node to itself
* [x] Call edge from unconnected implicit parameter node to call node

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Create new Sequence diagram 
2. Attempt one of the sequences above in developer mode
3. Observe uncaught exception

**Configuration**
 - JetUML Release: Integration 3.6 `b9a67beceb09104d5fb61d252e1037d6c1d47099`

I_kwDOAblrlc5LPqHf.Title: Refactor access to viewing logic
I_kwDOAblrlc5LPqHf.Author: prmr
I_kwDOAblrlc5LPqHf.Body: The various accesses to objects in charge of viewing diagrams and diagram elements is getting unprincipled due to the growing number of per-diagram special cases and optimizations. This should be collected under a single facade.

MDU6SXNzdWU1Mzc5Nzg1OA==.Title: Rip out the serialization support
MDU6SXNzdWU1Mzc5Nzg1OA==.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU1Mzc5Nzg1OA==.Body: The serialization of diagrams relies on the JavaBeans framework as opposed to binary serialization. It looks like we can get rid of all the binary serialization-related code.

Comments.Body: I removed all the dead code. Please try branch Issue10 and do as many different save/load scenarios you can think of, posting the outcome here (not the details unless a bug is found). If no issue is found, I'll merge the branch.
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body: Merged into master.
Comments.Author: prmr

I_kwDOAblrlc5Lam3z.Title: Simplify computation of isOverlapping
I_kwDOAblrlc5Lam3z.Author: prmr
I_kwDOAblrlc5Lam3z.Body: The simple check to see whether we need to shift a selection when pasting has disproportionate consequences on the design. It should be possible to make this check with a simpler algorithm, for example to see if any node in the selection has the same position as any node in the diagram.

I_kwDOAblrlc5719Lj.Title: Point node does not get deleted when corresponding edge is cascade-deleted
I_kwDOAblrlc5719Lj.Author: prmr
I_kwDOAblrlc5719Lj.Body:
**Description**
The point node connected to a note edge does not get deleted when the note _node_ gets deleted, triggering a cascade deletion of the edge. Then, a point node remains in the diagram, rendering it permanently invalid. When the edge is explicitly deleted, the point node is correctly deleted as well.

**Steps to reproduce**

1. Create new class diagram.
2. Create a note node.
3. Add a note edge from the note node to a point on the canvas.
4. Delete the note node.
5. Try to add a new node with notifications enabled.
6. The tool will notify that a point node remains in the diagram.

**Configuration**
 - JetUML Release: 3.6

MDU6SXNzdWUyODg2NzgwMTM=.Title: Division by zero on repeated edges between same nodes in StateDiagram
MDU6SXNzdWUyODg2NzgwMTM=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWUyODg2NzgwMTM=.Body:
Connecting two edges between the same start and end nodes in a State Diagram causes a division by zero. This is a regression bug because the functionality worked in Release 1.2. 

The bug is now triggered by test `TestUsageScenariosStateDiagram.testReconnectStatesWithPreExistingHorizontalEdgeShouldNotInduceDivisionByZeroException`

Comments.Body: Fixed by #225
Comments.Author: prmr

MDU6SXNzdWUyNzM1MTk5NTk=.Title: Decouple Graph from AWT API
MDU6SXNzdWUyNzM1MTk5NTk=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWUyNzM1MTk5NTk=.Body: The graph package should not rely on any GUI library, let alone AWT.

I_kwDOAblrlc5YCQ-T.Title: Disable pasting in sequence diagram
I_kwDOAblrlc5YCQ-T.Author: prmr
I_kwDOAblrlc5YCQ-T.Body: The semantics of pasting into a sequence diagram are ambiguous and limited. The tools validates that any sequence diagram represent a valid control flow. The implementation of `SequenceDiagramRenderer` requires at most one root call node. For this reason, the only pasting that really makes sense is of implicit parameter nodes. To make the behavior clearer, pasting into a sequence diagram will be disabled starting with 3.5

I_kwDOAblrlc6mq2EA.Author: prmr
I_kwDOAblrlc6mq2EA.Body: Small general maintenance, updates, and code cleanups not related to any feature or issue.

MDU6SXNzdWUxMjU1MjQ0NDc=.Author: prmr

MDU6SXNzdWUxMTkyODkwNzc=.Title: Adding multiple call edges moves call node lower
MDU6SXNzdWUxMTkyODkwNzc=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWUxMTkyODkwNzc=.Body:
Steps to reproduce:
1. Create a new sequence diagram
2. Create a call edge between two call nodes
3. Attempt to link the two same call nodes with the edge

No new edge will be created, which is fine, but for some reason the call nodes will move down.

MDU6SXNzdWUyODU0ODY3OTc=.Title: Eliminate MultiLineString
MDU6SXNzdWUyODU0ODY3OTc=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWUyODU0ODY3OTc=.Body: To have two versions of String, one mutable and one not, is a breeding ground for special cases. Also, the representation of strings should be handled by the `StringViewer`.

I_kwDOAblrlc5YDIXN.Title: Node Renderer Instance from DiagramRenderer instance
I_kwDOAblrlc5YDIXN.Author: Laurieprince
I_kwDOAblrlc5YDIXN.Body:
**Description**
In SequenceDiagramBuilder, a new ImplicitParamerRenderer instance is created from a new SequenceDiagramRenderer instance thus losing all information about the diagram renderer. Similarly, in DiagramBuilder, a new PackageNodeRenderer instance is created from a new DiagramRendererInstance.

I_kwDOAblrlc5mR5gT.Title: Add categorization to the reporting of deserialization problems
I_kwDOAblrlc5mR5gT.Author: prmr
I_kwDOAblrlc5mR5gT.Body:
Any kind of issue deserializing a JetUML file is currently reported as a `DeserializationException`, but there is a clear difference between:
* Syntactic errors (malformed Json)
* Structural errors (malformed diagram)
* Semantic errors (invalid diagram)

These should be reported as detected to facilitate integration with 3rd-party tools and libraries.

MDU6SXNzdWU3Mzc3NTE3MA==.Title: Sequence: Cutting an edge does not cut the corresponding return edge
MDU6SXNzdWU3Mzc3NTE3MA==.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU3Mzc3NTE3MA==.Body:
To reproduce:
1. Create two implicit parameter nodes, i1 and i2
2. Create a call edge between to call nodes, one on each of i1 and i2.
3. Add a return edge
3. Cut the call edge.
The return edge does not get cut (same with delete)

MDU6SXNzdWU1NDI4NjM0Nw==.Title: Add support for Activity Diagrams
MDU6SXNzdWU1NDI4NjM0Nw==.Author: EJBQ
MDU6SXNzdWU1NDI4NjM0Nw==.Body: Includes Scenario Start, Activities, Decisions, Synchronization, etc. See .21 or 2.0.1 for the rest.

Comments.Body: Park this one until someone actually requests it.
Comments.Author: prmr

MDU6SXNzdWUxNDMyNjM1NDM=.Title: Pasting partial sequence diagrams leads to crashes
MDU6SXNzdWUxNDMyNjM1NDM=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWUxNDMyNjM1NDM=.Body:
Steps to reproduce:
1. Create a sequence diagram with at least one call edge between two implicit parameter nodes
2. Select only the call edges and call nodes.
3. Copy the selection.
4. Open a new sequence diagram graph
5. Paste the selection

The application will hang.

The problem generalizes to pasting children nodes without their parent.

Comments.Body: Problem 1: The graph area grow/clip feature relied on a null value.
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body: Problem 2: The paste feature pasted children nodes with a reference to a parent node, when the parent node was not in the clip board. If children nodes are pasted without their parent, and this is a legal subgraph, the links to the (obsolete) parents should be clipped so the nodes can be made root nodes of the destination graph.
Comments.Author: prmr

MDU6SXNzdWUyNzQxODIxOTc=.Title: Separate Data and View concerns in Edge Hierarchy
MDU6SXNzdWUyNzQxODIxOTc=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWUyNzQxODIxOTc=.Body: In the current design `Edge` classes both support the basic representation of an edge in terms of start and end node, and provide all the logic to draw the edge. The edge drawing logic needs to be moved to a separate class, to facilitate transitioning to a new GUI framework. In the new design, `Edge` classes will supply a wrapper capable of drawing them using the call `edge.view()`, so e.g., to get the bounds of an edge one would do `edge.view().getBounds()` instead of `edge.getBounds()`.

MDU6SXNzdWUxMTg2NzcwMjA=.Title: Externalize editable property names
MDU6SXNzdWUxMTg2NzcwMjA=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWUxMTg2NzcwMjA=.Body: In `PropertySheet`. At the same time we can beautify the strings, e.g., `MiddleLabel` -> `Middle Label`

MDU6SXNzdWU0MTIwNDQ5OTg=.Title: JavaVersionNotDetected when using Early Access build of Java
MDU6SXNzdWU0MTIwNDQ5OTg=.Author: pashakondratyev
MDU6SXNzdWU0MTIwNDQ5OTg=.Body:
**Description**
JetUML will not startup if using an Early Access Build of Java
"Caused by: ca.mcgill.cs.jetuml.application.JavaVersionNotDetectedException: Cannot parse version string: 1.8.0_202-ea"

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Have an Early Access Build of Java
2. Try to run JetUML

**Configuration**
 - JetUML Release: 2.3
 - OS + version: Mac OS 10.14.1
 - Java version - 1.8.202-ea
 
**Additional information**
The issue has to do with the "parseWithOldNumberingScheme" method in the JavaVersion class as when the string "1.8.202-ea" is parsed the third token becomes 202-ea which cannot be read as an integer.

Comments.Body: We'll remove the version checking and put a notice on the website.
Comments.Author: prmr

MDU6SXNzdWUyODYzODMyMDk=.Title: Release 2.0a

I_kwDOAblrlc5UyC2t.Title: Binary distribution not available for MacOS
I_kwDOAblrlc5UyC2t.Author: Kabanets
I_kwDOAblrlc5UyC2t.Body:
The application can't start on macOS Ventura. 
We have a modal dialog with a text: “JetUML is damaged and can’t be opened. You should move it to the Bin", and two buttons "Move to Bin" & "Cancel".

Comments.Body:
## Update

We have identified this to be an issue with creating unsigned appplications with the `jpackage` tool. MacOS now requires external applications to be [signed with an Apple Developer ID](https://developer.apple.com/developer-id/).

Until this requirement can be properly funded and supported, the workaround is to run the application as a [thin jar](https://github.com/prmr/JetUML/blob/master/docs/install.md#thin-jar), which has been confirmed to work fine.
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body: Had the same problem, tried it with the thin jar and it opened up.
Comments.Author: tvut

Comments.Body: Thanks for confirming. I'll try to find a volunteer to package the application for this OS.
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body: That looks like you might have downloaded the wrong architecture for javafx? I'm using an M1 Air and I downloaded the latest aarch64 javafx build and it worked for me.
Comments.Author: tvut

Comments.Body: Another +1 for not working on M1/Ventura
Comments.Author: shayneoneill

Java(TM) SE Runtime Environment (build 19.0.2+7-44)
