MDU6SXNzdWU4MDY5MDQ4NjY=.Title: Refactor the StringViewer
MDU6SXNzdWU4MDY5MDQ4NjY=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU4MDY5MDQ4NjY=.Body: The `StringViewer` class used to draw strings on the canvas creates a very high number of `Text` objects at runtime, and returns overly conservative dimensions for the text. These two aspects can be improved.

MDU6SXNzdWU3ODU0Njg0ODQ=.Title: Edge labels for segmented edges shift when switching selection
MDU6SXNzdWU3ODU0Njg0ODQ=.Author: yannsartori
MDU6SXNzdWU3ODU0Njg0ODQ=.Body:
**Description**
If you have edge labels on segmented edges (aggregation, composition, or association) and there is at least one class with some attributes and at least one without, regardless of which classes you select, alternating between selecting one with some attributes versus one without moves the labels of all the segmented edges present on the diagram.
The correct behaviour should be it remains static.

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Create new class diagram
2. Create two classes
3. Connect the two with an aggregation edge, and label the edge components
4. Add an attribute to one of the classes
5. Switch between the two classes. See the labels moving.

**Configuration**
 - JetUML Release: 3.1
 - OS + version: Mac OS 11.1 and Windows 10
 - Java version OpenJDK 15

MDU6SXNzdWU1NTYxODc2OA==.Title: Remove Select Next and Select Previous features
MDU6SXNzdWU1NTYxODc2OA==.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU1NTYxODc2OA==.Body: @JoelChev, @EJBQ do you use this feature? This is a discussion about whether to remove it. I've never used it and axing it would greatly simplify the implementation of issue #36, which in turn is the preparation for #21

MDU6SXNzdWU1NTA5OTY4OTA=.Title: Add shortcut keys for tools
MDU6SXNzdWU1NTA5OTY4OTA=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU1NTA5OTY4OTA=.Body:
Tools in the toolbar should be accessible using shortcut keys.

The shortcuts are activated by typing the characters `1-9,0,A-Z` to select the tool, where 1 selects the first tool in the tool bar, etc. (0 represents 10 because it is located to the right of the 9 on most keyboards).

MDU6SXNzdWUxMjcxMTU1MDE=.Title: Milestone 0.10 Release
MDU6SXNzdWUxMjcxMTU1MDE=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWUxMjcxMTU1MDE=.Body: Update the version tag, write the release notes. Document the `ToolBar` feature. Release.

MDU6SXNzdWU0MDM0Njg0NDU=.Title: Exception when dragging after ctrl+click on selected node
MDU6SXNzdWU0MDM0Njg0NDU=.Author: aoldershaw
MDU6SXNzdWU0MDM0Njg0NDU=.Body:
**Description**
With a single node selected, if you attempt to drag on that node while holding the ctrl button (which deselects a node), a `java.util.NoSuchElementException` is thrown in the "JavaFX Application Thread".

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Create new class diagram 
2. Create a class node
3. Select the class node
4. While holding the ctrl key, click on the class node and drag the mouse
5. Exception is thrown

**Configuration**
 - JetUML Release: 2.3
 - OS + version: Mac OS 10.13.4
 - Java version: `Java(TM) SE Runtime Environment (build 1.8.0_111-b14)`
 
**Additional information**
With assertions disabled, the following exception is thrown:

```
Exception in thread "JavaFX Application Thread" java.util.NoSuchElementException: No value present
	at java.util.Optional.get(Optional.java:135)
	at ca.mcgill.cs.jetuml.gui.SelectionModel.getSelectionBounds(SelectionModel.java:86)
	at ca.mcgill.cs.jetuml.gui.DiagramCanvasController.computePointToReveal(DiagramCanvasController.java:517)
	at ca.mcgill.cs.jetuml.gui.DiagramCanvasController.mouseDragged(DiagramCanvasController.java:494)
	at ca.mcgill.cs.jetuml.gui.DiagramCanvasController.lambda$new$2(DiagramCanvasController.java:89)
	at com.sun.javafx.event.CompositeEventHandler.dispatchBubblingEvent(CompositeEventHandler.java:86)
	at com.sun.javafx.event.EventHandlerManager.dispatchBubblingEvent(EventHandlerManager.java:238)
	at com.sun.javafx.event.EventHandlerManager.dispatchBubblingEvent(EventHandlerManager.java:191)
	at com.sun.javafx.event.CompositeEventDispatcher.dispatchBubblingEvent(CompositeEventDispatcher.java:59)
	at com.sun.javafx.event.BasicEventDispatcher.dispatchEvent(BasicEventDispatcher.java:58)
	at com.sun.javafx.event.EventDispatchChainImpl.dispatchEvent(EventDispatchChainImpl.java:114)
	at com.sun.javafx.event.BasicEventDispatcher.dispatchEvent(BasicEventDispatcher.java:56)
	at com.sun.javafx.event.EventDispatchChainImpl.dispatchEvent(EventDispatchChainImpl.java:114)
	at com.sun.javafx.event.BasicEventDispatcher.dispatchEvent(BasicEventDispatcher.java:56)
	at com.sun.javafx.event.EventDispatchChainImpl.dispatchEvent(EventDispatchChainImpl.java:114)
	at com.sun.javafx.event.BasicEventDispatcher.dispatchEvent(BasicEventDispatcher.java:56)
	at com.sun.javafx.event.EventDispatchChainImpl.dispatchEvent(EventDispatchChainImpl.java:114)
	at com.sun.javafx.event.BasicEventDispatcher.dispatchEvent(BasicEventDispatcher.java:56)
	at com.sun.javafx.event.EventDispatchChainImpl.dispatchEvent(EventDispatchChainImpl.java:114)
	at com.sun.javafx.event.BasicEventDispatcher.dispatchEvent(BasicEventDispatcher.java:56)
	at com.sun.javafx.event.EventDispatchChainImpl.dispatchEvent(EventDispatchChainImpl.java:114)
	at com.sun.javafx.event.EventUtil.fireEventImpl(EventUtil.java:74)
	at com.sun.javafx.event.EventUtil.fireEvent(EventUtil.java:54)
	at javafx.event.Event.fireEvent(Event.java:198)
	at javafx.scene.Scene$MouseHandler.process(Scene.java:3757)
	at javafx.scene.Scene$MouseHandler.access$1500(Scene.java:3485)
	at javafx.scene.Scene.impl_processMouseEvent(Scene.java:1762)
	at javafx.scene.Scene$ScenePeerListener.mouseEvent(Scene.java:2494)
	at com.sun.javafx.tk.quantum.GlassViewEventHandler$MouseEventNotification.run(GlassViewEventHandler.java:380)
	at com.sun.javafx.tk.quantum.GlassViewEventHandler$MouseEventNotification.run(GlassViewEventHandler.java:294)
	at java.security.AccessController.doPrivileged(Native Method)
	at com.sun.javafx.tk.quantum.GlassViewEventHandler.lambda$handleMouseEvent$354(GlassViewEventHandler.java:416)
	at com.sun.javafx.tk.quantum.QuantumToolkit.runWithoutRenderLock(QuantumToolkit.java:389)

```

The contract of `SelectionModel.getSelectionBounds` is broken (in particular, the assertion `assert lastSelected.isPresent()`)

Comments.Body:
A simple fix would be to check for `aSelectionModel.isEmpty();` within the if statement:

https://github.com/prmr/JetUML/blob/5019f6a4cff915a164fc3b61597aebe3d181201e/src/ca/mcgill/cs/jetuml/gui/DiagramCanvasController.java#L492-L496

I_kwDOAblrlc5JafBm.Title: Endless loop when drawing class diagrams with interleaved control-flow
I_kwDOAblrlc5JafBm.Author: prmr
I_kwDOAblrlc5JafBm.Body:
**Description**
Attempting to create a sequence diagram where a call to a constructor is followed to a call to the same object after a call to another object leads to an endless loop.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6033492/167717275-c2fabd62-f0aa-45fd-a9fa-77709ca469d7.png)

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Create the sequence diagram above
2. Select the Call Edge tool
3. Drag a new call edge from the call node on `B` to the lifeline of  `C`
4. The diagram disappears and the tools enters an unstable state.

**Configuration**
 - JetUML Release: 3.3
 - OS + version: All
 - Java version: All 
 
**Additional information**
The issue is located in `CallNodeViewer`. The error is that the code to compute the y-position of the new call node requires to detect the max Y position of any call node of this implicit parameter, but this check includes the current call node, which triggers the endless loop.

Comments.Body: Fixed indirectly through the better design implemented with issue #470
Comments.Author: prmr

MDU6SXNzdWUxMjk3MzM1ODg=.Title: Re-size objects not possible.
MDU6SXNzdWUxMjk3MzM1ODg=.Author: chichoch
MDU6SXNzdWUxMjk3MzM1ODg=.Body:
When selecting an object, the corners are visible in such a way that they should be resizeable, which they aren't. 

For usability-reasons, it would be beneficial if the user actually could re-size objects.

Comments.Body:
This is designed as a feature. Nodes are drawn with an automatically-determined size that matches their contents as well as possible, hence saving the need for an additional sizing gesture.
However, if the nodes don't draw properly, that would be something to fix. In that case, please post the steps to reproduce.
Comments.Author: prmr

MDU6SXNzdWUxMjcwNTU0MDk=.Title: Sequence Diagram Self-Call Node Selection
MDU6SXNzdWUxMjcwNTU0MDk=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWUxMjcwNTU0MDk=.Body: In Sequence diagram with self-calls, when attempting to select a self-call node that is overlaid on top of its caller node, if the mouse is pressed at a point that is on the left side of the leaf node (so also overlapping the caller), then the caller will be selected instead of the leaf callee. The workaround is to ensure to select a point on the right of the caller node when selecting leaf called nodes. This should be fixed as part of an overhaul that adds a Z coordinate to nodes to properly manage who's on top.

MDU6SXNzdWU3ODg0NDE2OQ==.Title: Use Case Diagram Note Edge Between Non-Note Nodes
MDU6SXNzdWU3ODg0NDE2OQ==.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU3ODg0NDE2OQ==.Body: In use case diagrams it's possible to create node edges between two nodes that are both not note edges.

Comments.Body: Implemented in branch 29
Comments.Author: prmr

MDU6SXNzdWU1OTUzNjA1MzM=.Title: Test code dependent on OS
MDU6SXNzdWU1OTUzNjA1MzM=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU1OTUzNjA1MzM=.Body: Even though we defined an application font (see #181) to avoid the issue where different default system fonts on different systems would introduce different behavior for testing, there remains cases where the geometric calculations based on fonts still exhibit differences between systems.

MDU6SXNzdWUzMjEyNDk0NDE=.Title: Remove attributions from code
MDU6SXNzdWUzMjEyNDk0NDE=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWUzMjEyNDk0NDE=.Body: Remove all `@author` attribution lines. The information can be recovered from Git if necessary.

MDU6SXNzdWU0Njc0OTU4NjI=.Title: Make DiagramView stateless instead of a Wrapper
MDU6SXNzdWU0Njc0OTU4NjI=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU0Njc0OTU4NjI=.Body: Change the design so that an instance of `DiagramView` (renamed `DiagramViewer`) becomes a stateless Strategy for drawing diagrams. This will make the architecture simpler because the `Diagram` instance will be more prominently the owner of the diagram state. Also, it will facilitate later decoupling of `DiagramElement` from their views and the complete separation of the diagram logic from JavaFX.

MDU6SXNzdWUyOTU3ODcyMTg=.Title: Display too small on screen in 3000*2000 in 13inch
MDU6SXNzdWUyOTU3ODcyMTg=.Author: LotuxPunk
MDU6SXNzdWUyOTU3ODcyMTg=.Body:
Hello, 
I have a Surface Book, and my screen has a large resolution on a 13-inch screen, so the different elements in the interface look small. An option to adjust that? 

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/16562647/36017652-ed7eba82-0d78-11e8-830d-af24e67cafc6.png)

![2018-02-09](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/16562647/36017920-fc6963fc-0d79-11e8-8f05-daeb57677924.png)

Comments.Body:
This is a known issue caused by the fact that the current GUI library used (Swing) does not support HD screens. We are actively working on this with a port to JavaFX (#232 ), which will go into Release 2.0, scheduled for the Spring. In the meantime, two workarounds include:
* Using the Zoom feature (`View | Zoom In`) which at least makes the diagrams easier to see.
* (For the more adventurous) check out branch `Integration-JavaFX`, which already has the outer frame and dialogs ported, and will progressively become more HD-Screen friendly over the next few weeks. This will require compiling your own jar, but it should build out of the box in e.g., Eclipse.
Comments.Author: prmr

MDU6SXNzdWU2OTYxMTgyMjE=.Title: Remove Diagram parameter from edge segmentation algorithms
MDU6SXNzdWU2OTYxMTgyMjE=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU2OTYxMTgyMjE=.Body:
The parameter of type `Diagram` dates back to when `Edge` nodes did not store a reference to their containing diagram. This parameter can now be removed to simplify the code.

This issue is a special case of #230.

MDU6SXNzdWUzMjEyNzg4NDQ=.Title: Canvas focus is unnecessarily showing
MDU6SXNzdWUzMjEyNzg4NDQ=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWUzMjEyNzg4NDQ=.Body: When manipulating a diagram, a blue border around the canvas shows that it's in focus. This disappears when the mouse interactions are on the toolbar. Although not critical, this extraneous visual display is distracting: find a way to disable it.  This only seems to happen with Java 10, no 8.

MDU6SXNzdWU5NzQ5MDEzNzU=.Title: Release 3.3
MDU6SXNzdWU5NzQ5MDEzNzU=.Author: prmr

I_kwDOAblrlc52054Y.Title: Files with newlines cannot be opened
I_kwDOAblrlc52054Y.Author: prmr
I_kwDOAblrlc52054Y.Body:
**Description**
JetUML writes out a diagram in json format as a single line and expects to read it back in as a single line. This is unnecessarily brittle, especially when one wants to automatically format a diagram file to look at it. We should tolerate files with line breaks.

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Create a diagram
2. Save the diagram in a file
3. Open the file with a text editor and add a few line breaks that don't invalidate the json syntax or schema, e.g., between values and keys. Save the file.
4. Try to open the file with JetUML and a syntax error will be reported.

**Screenshots**
![image](https://github.com/prmr/JetUML/assets/6033492/826b9c2a-9d65-43a4-a4bd-c8c06eec5720)

**Configuration**
 - JetUML Release: 3.6

MDU6SXNzdWUyOTMyMjkxMzg=.Title: Bug in Zoom feature (regression)
MDU6SXNzdWUyOTMyMjkxMzg=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWUyOTMyMjkxMzg=.Body: The zoom in command has no effect, and the zoom out command hangs the application. This appears to be a regression caused by the refactoring to 2.0.alpha. This is to the switch of `GraphPanel.aZoom` from `double` to `int`. Take the opportunity to refactor Zoom into a properly separated abstraction.

MDU6SXNzdWU1NTM4MDY2Nzc=.Title: Convert Diagram Type hierarchy to enum
MDU6SXNzdWU1NTM4MDY2Nzc=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU1NTM4MDY2Nzc=.Body: Class `Diagram` is currently extended by classes that represent diagram types (e.g., `ClassDiagram`, `SequenceDiagram`. At this point the only purpose of these classes is to hold class-scope information about the type of diagram. This type of information would be better suited to a statically scoped structure. We'll put this into the enum type `DiagramType`.

MDU6SXNzdWUxNTAxMzYxMzA=.Title: Problems with pasting and redoing
MDU6SXNzdWUxNTAxMzYxMzA=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWUxNTAxMzYxMzA=.Body:
Steps to reproduce:
1. In a class diagram, create a graph with two nodes connected by an edge
2. Copy, paste
3. Undo
4. Redo
   The edge is not included in the redone action.

Comments.Body: Fixed as result of #151
Comments.Author: prmr

MDU6SXNzdWU4NzgzMTI2NQ==.Title: Package child nodes does not reappear in package with undo deletion
MDU6SXNzdWU4NzgzMTI2NQ==.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU4NzgzMTI2NQ==.Body:
Steps to reproduce:
1. Create a package node
2. Add a class node to it
3. Delete it
4. Undo

All fine
1. Add another node to the package
2. Delete that new node
3. Undo

The node reappears outside the package.

Comments.Body: Fixed as part of the #106 refactoring.
Comments.Author: prmr

MDU6SXNzdWU1NTcxNTc2Nzc=.Title: Tip of the day feature
MDU6SXNzdWU1NTcxNTc2Nzc=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU1NTcxNTc2Nzc=.Body:
Document the key features of JetUML as documentation modules (a.k.a. "tips"), that can be viewed one by one when the tool starts, or as a whole from the help menu. A user preference should determine whether tips are shown upon startup. 

Each tip should correspond to one cohesive piece of documentation about the tool. Tips should be stored as resources, and comprise at least one block of mark-up text and optionally one image. It should be possible to generate an indexed documentation page by using the documentation.

The documentation modules should have a sequence index. The JetUML properties should remember the last module seen by the user.

MDU6SXNzdWUzMjU5MTE3NzU=.Title: Modernize the Help Menu

MDU6SXNzdWUyOTE1NTkzMTE=.Title: PointNode does not auto-delete when note edge deleted
MDU6SXNzdWUyOTE1NTkzMTE=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWUyOTE1NTkzMTE=.Body:
When deleting a note edge connected to a Point node, the Point node does not get deleted. This is the default behavior for all nodes. However, in the case of a Point node, it makes no sense to leave it in, so it should be deleted whenever its corresponding note edge is deleted.

**Steps to reproduce**:
1. Create a new class diagram
2. Create a Class Node and a Note node
3. Create a new note edge by starting from the note node and ending at an arbitrary location (not within the class node)
4. Select only the edge and delete it
5. Select All

The selection will show the remaining point node.

Comments.Body: In `Graph.removeEdge(Edge)`, `if( n instanceof NoteEdge )` should be `if( n instanceof NoteNode)`.
Comments.Author: prmr

I_kwDOAblrlc5UXPRx.Title: Small visual glitch with aggregation line in class diagram
I_kwDOAblrlc5UXPRx.Author: cjoudrey
I_kwDOAblrlc5UXPRx.Body: **Description** I was in the process of drawing a class diagram with two arrows pointing between two objects and noticed a slight visual glitch. It's not a big deal, but I figured I'd open an issue in case it is a low hanging fruit. **Screenshots** Notice how the aggregate line isn't fully straight: **Configuration** - JetUML Release: 3.4 - OS + version: macOS 12.6 - Java version: 17.0.4.1

Comments.Body:
The root cause is the specification of the behavior of `NodeIndex#toPoint`, which computes attachment points on a node face attempting to maximize the space available on the attached face of the node. As observed, the excess precision can lead to a suboptimal look.

I will change the function so that the outcome is more visually pleasing.
Comments.Author: prmr

I_kwDOAblrlc52de5A.Title: Feature for underlining and italics?
I_kwDOAblrlc52de5A.Author: WhyRoX
I_kwDOAblrlc52de5A.Body:
Hi, I have been using JetUML for my studies. However, I need to underline text and the feature isn't available. Would it be possible to add it? 

Thank you

Comments.Body: Currently looking into it.
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body:
Users can now **underline** and **italicize** their static and abstract elements, respectively, using markups.

- Underlining markup: Surround the desired element with underscores. This is only allowed for class attributes and methods.
E.g.  \_getArea(): double\_
- Italics markup: Surround the desired element with forward slashes. This is only allowed for class names and methods.
E.g.  /Shape/

Notes: 
1. _Any_ leading or trailing spaces before or after the markups will result in the markup not being applied. 
2. A combination of underlining and italicizing is not supported since an element cannot be both static and abstract. More precisely,
/_ XXX _/ would make the element abstract and
\_/ XXX /\_ would make it underlined.
Only the outside decoration would be applied.
Comments.Author: jkim7x

@jidicula  can you put this somewhere and try `java -jar test.zip`? This is the simplest possible executable jar.
[test.zip](https://github.com/prmr/JetUML/files/4173268/test.zip)

Comments.Body: OK thanks. The issue is most likely that your JRE does not have the JavaFX binaries. The second version of `test.zip` simply had a reference to a JavaFX class. I'll be closing this since it's a Java config problem and not a JetUML issue. To fix the problem, make sure your have openjfx installed and on the classpath. Also FYI, Release 3.0 is coming out soon and with it we'll be distributing JetUML as a fat jar precisely to avoid these kinds of issues.

MDU6SXNzdWU2NTc1NTA4Nw==.Title: Add edge list into nodes
MDU6SXNzdWU2NTc1NTA4Nw==.Author: EJBQ
MDU6SXNzdWU2NTc1NTA4Nw==.Body: We need to make sure this is transient when saving, but can still be loaded back in

Comments.Body: @EJBQ I'm looking into this a bit right now. I just want to clarify the intention here a bit. As it stands, if there is an edge that goes as follows A ---> B (where A is the origin node and B is the ending node), then it is only "added" through A, it's origin. Would you like the implementation to be set up in such a way that when you add an edge it is stored in the transient ArrayList of Node A AND Node B, or just Node A? Thanks!
Comments.Author: JoelChev

Comments.Body:
Hmm. I think it would be better if it were added to both Node A and Node B. And it's simple enough to test if the node is the start or the end, so we can keep them all in one list.

Adding to both nodes let you remove a edge from the selection automatically that goes into the node you are removing, which is better.
Comments.Author: EJBQ

Comments.Body: I have implemented an initial design with two transient ArrayLists of Edges (origin and terminal edges). I can modify this if needed, but I need to do some work testing it for the time being.
Comments.Author: JoelChev

I_kwDOAblrlc6H-oHg.Title: Run geometry tests only on windows
I_kwDOAblrlc6H-oHg.Author: prmr
I_kwDOAblrlc6H-oHg.Body: The geometry tests ensure that the diagram renders properly. For this purpose it uses pixel coordinates. Because of system fonts and other environment configuration details, these coordinates vary from OS to OS. The current solution is to add values for all 3 main OSes, which is extremely cumbersome and does not provide much value because the underlying geometry calculations are OS-independent. Instead, we will run the tests only on Windows.

MDU6SXNzdWU0MTE5Mjg1NTE=.Title: Refactor Creation of Tool Icons
MDU6SXNzdWU0MTE5Mjg1NTE=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU0MTE5Mjg1NTE=.Body:
The toolbar and context menu show icons of the nodes and edges supported by a diagram. These images are created by drawing the tool's corresponding view. There are two problems with the current design:
* The view code requires special cases just to deal with icons (e.g., `CallEdgeView`)
* The `ImageCreator` creates an image from a `Canvas`, which is suboptimal because in JavaFX it is possible to just pass the `Canvas` itself as an icon in a menu.

**Refactoring**: Create an `IconCreator` interface extended by the view interfaces and a special method in the views, and use that to create icons instead of `View.draw(...)`. 

**Note**: Some widgets, and in particular the context menu, disposes of its images when it's closed, so any reference to an image passed to it cannot be shared.

MDU6SXNzdWU3MjI2MDk0MQ==.Title: Unit tests for class Clipboard
MDU6SXNzdWU3MjI2MDk0MQ==.Author: prmr

Without calling this method, the clipboard class iterates through all of the possible edges for a given diagram and pastes a copy of each (as there is no simple .equals() method right now for a given edge type). Some edges (in Use Case Diagrams for instance) have a middle label like "<<include>>" which I need to compare against the list of Edges for a given diagram.

This means it's hard for me to create the Commands so that I can pass just one or the other. The issue is that calling the method in the Graph (where adding nodes and edges is) has no way to refer to its parent GraphPanel at the moment. And if I don't pass a reference to GraphPanel, then I can't repaint the scene after my action and it remains still until you change something else. @prmr and @JoelChev , do you think I should just add a reference to GraphPanel for now or refactor in some better way?

Many of the `ChildNode` implementation use nullable values to represent the absence of a parent, which could be improved through API redesign.

MDU6SXNzdWU1Mzk2NzI0NA==.Body: Since the refactoring of the graph framework, properties appear in a strange order in the properties editor. MDU6SXNzdWU5MDc0NDA5NTA=.Title: Snapping Node Dimensions to Grid, except when Moving
MDU6SXNzdWU5MDc0NDA5NTA=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU5MDc0NDA5NTA=.Body:
Currently, the position of the nodes is snapped to the grid, but their dimensions (width and height) are not. 

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6033492/120195487-dbdbf480-c1ec-11eb-91c7-751a4a89aef3.png)

It might be interesting to find a way to snap the dimensions of the nodes to the grid as well. However, this can be tricky, because simply snapping the diagram elements' bounds in the viewer creates an undesirable "jitter" effect when moving the nodes. For a really smooth experience, the nodes' view should not be snapped when moving them. 

One design idea is to snap the bounding box to the grid multiple while moving. This will naturally snap to grid once the position is snapped to grid.

I_kwDOAblrlc5ogtou.Title: Detect deserialization errors when launching with files argument
I_kwDOAblrlc5ogtou.Author: prmr
I_kwDOAblrlc5ogtou.Body:
**Description**
JetUML supports opening with file passed as command-line argument, which in turns supports the "open with" feature of certain OSes (see #382). If the file cannot be open for any reason, the application silently fails. The error should be reported in the same way as is done when opening the file from a running JetUML application.

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Create an invalid JetUML file
2. Start JetUML passing this file as argument
3. In user mode, launching the app fails silently. In developer mode, an exception stack trace gets dumped on the console.

**Configuration**
 - JetUML Release: 3.5
 - OS + version: All
 - Java version: All
 
**Additional information**
Attempt to open the file separately from launching the editor in `EditorFrame`, then launch (show) an Alert Dialog before exiting if there's an issue.

MDU6SXNzdWU4MjgxOTQ3MjE=.Title: Refactor code involving text to use StringViewer
MDU6SXNzdWU4MjgxOTQ3MjE=.Author: yannsartori
MDU6SXNzdWU4MjgxOTQ3MjE=.Body:
Currently, there are a couple classes that contain operations involving text (`AbstractEdgeViewer`, `StateTransitionEdgeViewer`, etc) which all reimplement functionalities provided by StringViewer. These areas can be rewritten to utilize StringViewer

The result of this refactoring would make adding features involving text much easier, since there is only one point of contact.

MDU6SXNzdWUzMTI0ODE2MzE=.Title: Overhaul the ToolBar
MDU6SXNzdWUzMTI0ODE2MzE=.Author: kkutschera
MDU6SXNzdWUzMTI0ODE2MzE=.Body:
Originally titled: **Toolbar Jitter in JavaFX**
When opening a diagram for the first time the toolbar jitters. This may be caused by the computation of preferred sizes when a `GraphFrame` is first created.

This issue is expanded to generally improve the Toolbar class, to:
* Take advantage of the new JavaFX `ToolBar` class
* Refactor the code, reduce state
* Eliminate the jitters upon initialization
* Improve the look and feel and give it a more modern look
* Select whether to view the tool hints or not through the View menu instead of an expansion button.
* Persist the view options #243

MDU6SXNzdWUxNDMyNjU0NjY=.Body: A feature that selects all root nodes and all edges. Available from the Edit menu and through a short-cut.

MDU6SXNzdWUxMjQxNTE3MTM=.Title: Support multiple connection points per node side in Class Diagram
MDU6SXNzdWUxMjQxNTE3MTM=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWUxMjQxNTE3MTM=.Body: When more than one edge connects to a connection point, the resulting edge can be confounded and difficult to see. Different edges should attach to different connection points on a node.

Comments.Body: One solution would be to change `Node.getConnectionPoint` so that it returns a connection points that takes into account the relative position of other nodes w.r.t. the implicit parameter. Unfortunately, that would also require the implicit parameter Node object somehow obtaining a reference to the `Graph` that contains it, which would induce a cycle of dependencies. It might be tolerable to keep this around only in concrete `Node` classes that need the information.
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body: Considering passing a `Graph` argument to `Edge.draw`. Experiment with `DependencyEdge`

Comments.Body: The self-edge needs to be dynamic as well

Comments.Body: `SegmentationStyle.getPath` needs to take the edge as argument, otherwise the `Position` calculation is too difficult to implement for pairs of nodes that have different types of edges between them.

Comments.Body: @prmr I have been looking at this problem a bit and I think I may need to implement a means of converting a SelectionList to a Graph. Do you think this will be necessary, or would there be an easier way to copy and paste the contents of a GraphPanel? I'm trying to work from the copy+paste feature of Violet 0.23 right now as a template, but their method is based on a Graph being the object created on a mouse-drag selection, not GraphElements being added to a SelectionList. Any tips or pointers would be appreciated.

Comments.Body: There's only one graph per panel, so you would want to go through the selection list and add the elements in the selection to the existing graph (and remove them from the original one in the case of cut). They are cloneable so that part should not be too hard. However, the more interesting bit here is to carefully specify the behavior of cut and paste. For instance, if should only work for nodes, but copying multiple nodes should include the edges between them, etc.
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body: Actually the cut part should just work because `GraphPanel.removeSelected()` does the right thing. However, implementing the pasting behavior will require a more systematic study. Don't base yourself on the 0.21 behavior because it's highly unsatisfactory. For example, if you cut an edge and paste it, a node appears!

Comments.Body: @prmr I just wrote a wiki page describing the behaviour as I see it. If you think I am missing anything or if you want anything else added, please feel free to edit it. I will think about this problem some more, but I think some major redesign work may be necessary for the pasting part of the operation (I don't believe copying will be too difficult to implement).
Comments.Author: JoelChev

Comments.Body: I'm not sure we need a big redesign. IMO inheriting `Clipboard` from `Graph` as in 0.23 is overkill for our needs. I've just committed some code to branch 0012 that sketches a solution. Look at class `Clipboard` and methods `cut`, `copy`, and `paste` in `EditorFrame`. Implementing these, and the required services in `GraphPanel`, should get you closer to something quite workable.

JoelChev

Comments.Author: JoelChev

Comments.Body:
# Parent-Child Relation in Sequence Diagrams

(this comment is also relevant to @EJBQ 's work)
It seems a lot of the friction you are experiencing comes from the fact that in sequence diagrams the parent-child relation is defined to map to the parent-child node in the corresponding call graph. This is not actually what we really need for a diagram editor. Instead, in all diagrams the parent-child node relation should be defined as "child node is semantically a component of the parent) (e.g., field nodes in the object diagram). 
I started a wiki page to document the parent-child relations
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body:
@prmr @EJBQ I forgot to tag my recent commits with a #12 (my bad, I will do this again from now on). In my recent two commits, I have fixed two major bugs:
1. The duplication/buggy behavior of Field Nodes that lay inside ObjectNodes when they are cloned. Now the copying/cutting and pasting behaves as it should for this diagram.
2. The weird bug that prevented diagrams from being translated if an edge was in the SelectionList. I had to modify the SelectionList class to make this work properly, essentially, it needed a getLastNode() method not a getLastSelected() method (as an Edge can be the last element in the SelectionList). Take a look at my additions to the GraphPanel and SelectionList classes if this seems confusing.

Anyways, I encourage you guys to check out my latest commit to this branch and play around with it and make sure both issues have been fixed. I will be working on fixing the Sequence Diagram bugs between now and our next meeting. Thanks!
Comments.Author: JoelChev

Comments.Body: I have taken a look into the Sequence Diagram issue a bit, and it is still giving me issues. I think we need to decide as a group if we want to redesign this or not. As you mentioned @prmr, CallNodes can have CallNode children (which to me seems like an odd way to organize things). I would much rather see CallNodes by default be children of ImplicitParameterNodes (unless they are created by a self-call). I don't know what you guys think about this, but I am open to discussing this at our next meeting in further detail.
Comments.Author: JoelChev

Comments.Body: While testing I found a new issue with pasting and the undo system, as well as an issue with double pasting. I'm delaying the release by a day while I fix this.

Comments.Body: The undoing issue is solved, just waiting on @JoelChev to look at double pasting.

If either of you can please check out #13's latest commit and play around with double pasting nodes with edges (so copy/cut nodes with edges between them, then paste them two or more times in a row), I would appreciate it. I_kwDOAblrlc7G_Hhv.Title: Add Maven Build Configuration
I_kwDOAblrlc7G_Hhv.Author: prmr
I_kwDOAblrlc7G_Hhv.Body:
Add support for building the project with Maven as an alternative to default building with Eclipse. 

* [ ] The project should remain compilable and runnable in Eclipse with no additional configuration.
* [ ] The build configuration (`pom.xml`) should respect the current directory structure.
* [ ] The build configuration should include options for packaging JetUML as a thin or fat jar, or as a native application installer.

We can also use this opportunity to migrate to Java 25 when it's out.

MDU6SXNzdWU1NTcxNTIzMTE=.Title: Modernize Icons
MDU6SXNzdWU1NTcxNTIzMTE=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU1NTcxNTIzMTE=.Body:
Update the icons, drawing from a single icon set. We could use [Oxygen icons](https://github.com/pasnox/oxygen-icons-png). Suggestions welcome. The license should not require attribution, so that we have one less thing to take care of.

Make sure to update the picture on the readme (which shows some icons).

MDU6SXNzdWU2NjM3MzQ4Mw==.Title: Select Element After Creation
MDU6SXNzdWU2NjM3MzQ4Mw==.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU2NjM3MzQ4Mw==.Body: Currently when you want to create a model element and name it (e.g., a class), you have to create it, select the selection tool, select it, then click the name field. I have a tendency to try to do this which results in the annoying behavior that multiple classes get created. Instead, if a selection tool is active but the mouse is clicked inside an object that cannot be the parent of the created element, the object clicked should be selected and it's property editor opened instead.

Comments.Body: The focus part is handled in #69
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body: I have added logic now so that if you click on a Node, it will switch the tool to be the Selection tool instead. There are currently three exceptions to this rule: PackageNodes, ObjectNodes and ImplicitParameterNodes (as these nodes can contain other nodes inside them). Now, when a user clicks on a node twice, it will display its property editor for them, with the first field selected.
Comments.Author: JoelChev

MDU6SXNzdWU1NTE1NjI1MDc=.Title: Application crash caused by JavaFX bug
MDU6SXNzdWU1NTE1NjI1MDc=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU1NTE1NjI1MDc=.Body:
## Problem
Some MacOS users have reported both predictable and unpredictable crashes when using the application, and in particular in relation to the management of windows and frames. These includes:
* When opening a second diagram (#300)
* When switching ("tabbing") between the JetUML application and other system applications (#322)

## Likely Cause
As far as I could determine this is due to incompatibilities and/or unstable behavior in the underlying JavaFX binaries. The most likely cause is [this JDK bug](https://bugs.openjdk.java.net/browse/JDK-8211304). Similar documented cases include:
* Not using an [Oracle certified system configuration](https://www.oracle.com/technetwork/java/javase/certconfig-2095354.html), see [Stack Overflow: Javafx crashes on OSX on JRE 1.8](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/34711128)
* Running the OS on VMWare [JDK Bug Report: Fatal error when running JavaFX application on OSX running on Vmware](https://bugs.openjdk.java.net/browse/JDK-8146952)

See [this Stack Overflow answer](https://stackoverflow.com/a/52586569) for more details.

## Workaround
This bug has been fixed in Java 12 and is being backported to earlier versions. Please try updating your system to see if it fixes the problem.

## Related Issues:
* #280 Potential System Incompatibilities on MacOS
* #300 Opening second diagram crashes on Mac
* #322 Application crashing on window de-focus
* #385 Unable to open diagram with large Canvas

Comments.Body: This has not been reported for 3.0+. Assuming it's either resolved by a JavaFX backport or subsumed by #385
Comments.Author: prmr

MDU6SXNzdWU4NzEzNDMyOTY=.Title: Refactor node position so that x and y are no longer properties
MDU6SXNzdWU4NzEzNDMyOTY=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU4NzEzNDMyOTY=.Body:
In the design of diagram `Node` elements, the node's position is stored as a two node properties, `x` and `y` respectively. This has the advantage of simplifying the encoding and decoding of diagrams, since position values get serialized like all other node attributes. However, this insignificant advantage comes at a great cost:

1. The `x` and `y` properties are "special" because they should not be handled by the property sheet, thus requiring a whole mechanism to handle _invisible_ properties.
2. It means the `Point` class cannot be immutable (as it should), because properties have to be mutable.

We can greatly simplify the design of the code by not storing a node's position using properties.

I_kwDOAblrlc4--7Xv.Title: Improvements to the Edge Label Alignments
I_kwDOAblrlc4--7Xv.Author: delphinepilon
I_kwDOAblrlc4--7Xv.Body: Some points could be improved in the layout and alignment of edge labels: 1. In the state diagrams, state transition edges have a fixed line length when there are multiple words in the edge label. This can be inconvenient as it is less flexible than dynamic line lengths. https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/43301868/142290587-ed2247e8-bfb9-4879-8dd8-ba50ee76c063.mov 2. In class diagrams, segmented edges can have three labels. For horizontal segmented edges, the labels that are closest to the nodes are slightly shifted away from the node to clear the arrowhead. This is not the case for vertical segmented edges. Here, by horizontal segmented edge, I mean that the majority of segments are horizontal. 3. In class diagrams, the middle label of segmented edges can sometimes overlap the segments themselves which is not ideal as it can make the labels less readable.

Comments.Body: Fixed with PR #447
Comments.Author: prmr

MDU6SXNzdWU5NzU3NDY2ODc=.Title: Refactor the PersistenceTests
MDU6SXNzdWU5NzU3NDY2ODc=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU5NzU3NDY2ODc=.Body:
There are a number of problems with the tests in `TestPersistenceService`:

* The tests do two things, test that the persistence works, and verify that the diagrams are drawn as expected. This should be split up.
* The part that verifies the diagram is not that useful. It just tests the bounds of all diagram elements. If something goes wrong, we don't really know what except that the pixels are off. We should test for specific expectations about the absolute and/or relative positions and dimensions of objects.
* The part that verifies the diagram is sensitive to the OS that the test runs on, because different system fonts lead to slightly different dimensions. See #346 and #400. It's a huge pain to have to adapt the tests for all OSes. The same oracle should be valid independently of the OS.

Comments.Body:
Linking two additional commits: 
- 782dbd7fe7ee87098f2f7710a1924dcf3746aa67 
- 86122960323b506ca80a56d246d7cc995c65cd36
Comments.Author: delphinepilon

MDU6SXNzdWU1NjkwNDk3MTU=.Title: Moving package nodes using child nodes behaves incorrectly
MDU6SXNzdWU1NjkwNDk3MTU=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU1NjkwNDk3MTU=.Body:
**Description**
When moving a package node by moving the underlying child node, some of the behavior is not intuitive:
1. If the child node is deleted, the package node repositions itself to its original position. It should stay at its current position. 
2. Normally moving a child node within a package node should result in moving the node in the left and up directions, and enlarging the package node in the right and bottom directions. However, currently the node is moved in the right and bottom directions up until some point, at which point its starts becoming enlarged. This is probably based on the node's original position.

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Create new class diagram, with a package node and a child class node
2. Move the class node to the left
3. Delete the class node
4. The package node jumps to its original position

1. Create new class diagram, with a package node and a child class node
2. Move the class node to the left
3. Move the class node to the right. The original package node follows
4. Keep moving the node towards the right.
5. The package node eventually starts to enlarge.

**Configuration**
 - JetUML Release: 2.5
 - OS + version: All versions
 - Java version: All versions

Comments.Body:
As a solution, we will be changing the behavior of how the `PackageNode` gets drawn. The box for the package node will not clip to the bounding box of all its children, in all cases. It will thus no longer be possible to create drawings such as:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6033492/77580777-f7de5a80-6eb2-11ea-8729-bc25c894b6fd.png)

Instead, this diagram would look like:

![test](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6033492/77580903-23f9db80-6eb3-11ea-8dd3-8b5a2d754a77.png)
Comments.Author: prmr

MDU6SXNzdWUyNzUxNzU4MzU=.Title: Remove BoxedValue feature for Object Diagrams
MDU6SXNzdWUyNzUxNzU4MzU=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWUyNzUxNzU4MzU=.Body: The complexity required to support this feature is not worth the benefit as it requires data-flow between parent and child nodes. Consider an automatic rendering as boxed value of non-reference values.

MDU6SXNzdWU3MjI1OTM0Ng==.Title: Package nodes don't draw properly
MDU6SXNzdWU3MjI1OTM0Ng==.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU3MjI1OTM0Ng==.Body:
Creating a package node in a class diagram with something in it, then trying to:
- Move it
- copy/paste it
- Add more stuff in it

All lead to some erratic behavior. This issue was in 0.4, and I suspect perhaps even in the original code base, but that's nevertheless something we need to fix.

When moving a package node, the outline of the node does not become visible until the node is dropped. 

In `PackageNode` the `aTop` and `aBottom`  rectangles don't get refreshed when the node is translated. They should be computed on the fly in getShape(); Similarly, when the node is snapped, the top and bottom rectangles are not translated.

I_kwDOAblrlc50A-wy.Title: Deprecated Checkstyle checks in Style.xml
I_kwDOAblrlc50A-wy.Author: ArthusWQZ
I_kwDOAblrlc50A-wy.Body:
The following checks are deprecated:
- `GenericIllegalRegexp`: It was [removed in release 5.0](https://checkstyle.sourceforge.io/releasenotes_old_1-0_5-9.html#Release_5.0) and was replaced by [`RegexpMultiline`](https://checkstyle.sourceforge.io/checks/regexp/regexpmultiline.html#RegexpMultiline), [`RegexpSingleline`](https://checkstyle.sourceforge.io/checks/regexp/regexpsingleline.html#RegexpSingleline) and [`RegexpSinglelineJava`](https://checkstyle.sourceforge.io/checks/regexp/regexpsinglelinejava.html#RegexpSinglelineJava) _(the latter was added in release 6.0)_
- `PackageHtml`: Also removed in release 5.0, replaced with [`JavadocPackage`](https://checkstyle.sourceforge.io/checks/javadoc/javadocpackage.html#JavadocPackage) (**NOTE:** `PackageHtml` needed to be under the module `Treewalker` but `PackageHtml` [needs to be under `Checker`](https://checkstyle.sourceforge.io/checks/javadoc/javadocpackage.html#Parent_Module))

This information can be found at the bottom of the [release notes for version 5.0](https://checkstyle.sourceforge.io/releasenotes_old_1-0_5-9.html#Release_5.0).

I am using Checkstyle for IntelliJ IDEA, version 10.12.3 and these checks make the Style.xml rule file unreadable (Checkstyle crashes when trying to read it).

I_kwDOAblrlc5ho8Ht.Title: Refactor Semantic Validation
I_kwDOAblrlc5ho8Ht.Author: prmr
I_kwDOAblrlc5ho8Ht.Body:
JetUML validates that diagrams created in the application are semantically valid. This is currently support by the `DiagramBuilder` functionality, which prevents the addition of nodes and edges that violate semantic _constraints_. There are three problems with the current design:

1. Diagram files loaded in are not semantically validated. Therefore, it is possible to hand-craft (or used tools) to make a file that describes an invalid diagram, and load it in.
2. The constraint validation code cannot easily be reused to do batch validation of existing diagrams.
3. The constraint validation code does not easily support providing useful error messages when validation rules are not respected.

We need to refactor the constraint validation code so that it is more modular, reusable, and extensive to support the above scenarios.

The current design is to have the following workflow for all validation scenarios:

1. Create or update a diagram without any constrain validation;
2. Run all the validation rules, record violations
3. If there are any violations, undo the last update command.

MDU6SXNzdWUxMTAwMDQ1MTY=.Title: Smarter detection of graph dirty status
MDU6SXNzdWUxMTAwMDQ1MTY=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWUxMTAwMDQ1MTY=.Body:
Currently sequences of operations that do not change the status of a graph still result in a graph getting flagged as "dirty" (i.e., with unsaved modifications). These include:
- Undoing all operations 

It would be best if the tool could detect cases where no real modifications have been done to avoid flagging the graph as dirty.

Comments.Body: Solved by moving all the logic to determine whether a diagram has unmodified changes to `DiagramOperationProcessor` and delegating the required calls to this class. The current solution does not show visual feedback about whether a diagram is saved or not. Doing so would require another instance of the Observer to allow `DiagramTab` to listen to changes in `DiagramOperationProcessor`. It's not clear that the feature is worth this increase in code structures.
Comments.Author: prmr

MDU6SXNzdWUyODk2NDA0Njg=.Title: Improve use of Point class
MDU6SXNzdWUyODk2NDA0Njg=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWUyODk2NDA0Njg=.Body: There is inconsistent use of the overloaded constructor: sometimes the conversion to ints is done before the call sometimes after.

MDU6SXNzdWU1MDkxMDI3NzU=.Title: Ignored NullPointerException when no tool selected
MDU6SXNzdWU1MDkxMDI3NzU=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU1MDkxMDI3NzU=.Body:
**Description**
When a diagram tab is first opened, no tool is selected. The event handling code incorrectly assumes there is one, so when the user clicks on the canvas or attempts to copy the diagram to the clipboard, an uncaught `NullPointerException` is raised. Because it happens in the event loop, there is no other observable behavior, so this problem can only detected when JetUML is associated with a console.

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Open a diagram tab by creating a new diagram or opening a saved diagram.
2. Click on the canvas.

The following exception is raised:

```
Exception in thread "JavaFX Application Thread" java.lang.NullPointerException
	at ca.mcgill.cs.jetuml.gui.DiagramTabToolBar.getCreationPrototype(DiagramTabToolBar.java:177)
	at ca.mcgill.cs.jetuml.gui.DiagramCanvasController.getTool(DiagramCanvasController.java:378)
	at ca.mcgill.cs.jetuml.gui.DiagramCanvasController.handleSingleClick(DiagramCanvasController.java:323)
```

If for step 2 instead of clicking on the canvas, the "To Clipboard" button is used, the exception is:

```
Exception in thread "JavaFX Application Thread" java.lang.NullPointerException
	at ca.mcgill.cs.jetuml.gui.DiagramTabToolBar.lambda$0(DiagramTabToolBar.java:121)
	at com.sun.javafx.event.CompositeEventHandler.dispatchBubblingEvent(CompositeEventHandler.java:86)
```

**Configuration**
 - JetUML Release:  2.4 + 90aeca1
 - OS + version: Windows 10
 - Java version:  1.8.0u221
 
**Additional information**
The simplest solution is to enable the Selection Tool by default.

MDU6SXNzdWU5MjE2Mjg0OQ==.Title: Make ImplicitParameterNodes parent nodes
MDU6SXNzdWU5MjE2Mjg0OQ==.Author: prmr

`ImplicitParameterNode` should keep a list of all its call nodes and treat them as children. Also, cleanup the `Graph.addNode` and `Graph.removeNode` logic.

MDU6SXNzdWUxMjUxNzkxOTY=.Title: Make it possible to add existing nodes to packages
MDU6SXNzdWUxMjUxNzkxOTY=.Author: prmr

MDU6SXNzdWUzODIwMzU1NDA=.Title: Diagram too large error prevents diagram opening
MDU6SXNzdWUzODIwMzU1NDA=.Author: neilernst
MDU6SXNzdWUzODIwMzU1NDA=.Body: **Description** Diagram too large for view dialog does not open diagram, but forces you to resize then re-open. **Steps to reproduce** 1. Open a diagram saved on a higher resolution screen. 2. Error dialog (attached) shows dimensions are wrong and suggests resetting canvas size. 3. JetUML then refuses to open diagram. **Screenshots** **Configuration** - JetUML Release: 2.2 - OS + version: Mac 10.14 - Java version 1.8 **Additional information** I expected the tool to automatically resize the canvas and add scroll bars. Since it knows the solution, it seems like the tool should just go ahead and change. This is a particular problem when working on a laptop and opening multiple student projects, some of which are saved on larger screens.

Comments.Body:
This is a good suggestion. The rationale for the current behavior is twofold:
1. Canvas is not resizable
2. The size of the canvas was supposed to be a "view" attribute (in the sense of MVC), whereas the persistence feature was persisting only the model (i.e., the essential diagram components).

Given the annoyance that this causes, it might make sense to add the view size to the persistence file, and auto-size to it. 

The only issue is that storing the dimension of the canvas used to create a diagram will seriously uglify the design, since commands and open/save operate on a `Diagram`, not a `DiagramCanvas` or a `DiagramView`. Would it be acceptable to open the diagram with the minimum size required, plus a margin?
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body: that seems like the right approach to me - but how is minimum size defined? I think that the default (using Photoshop as an example) would be either "1:1" (and nodes might be off screen) or "fit", and for large diagrams, you see all the nodes, but perhaps at tiny sizes.
Comments.Author: neilernst

Comments.Body:
The (minimum) bounds are generated dynamically, using
```
Rectangle bounds = DiagramType.newViewInstanceFor(diagram).getBounds();
```
So that will be the minimum size. I'll add a margin to the left/bottom
Comments.Author: prmr

MDU6SXNzdWU1MzY3NTQ2Mg==.Title: Test class ExtensionFilter

MDU6SXNzdWUxNDM0OTcyMjE=.Title: Refactor graph creation behavior to distinguish between validated and non-validated operations
MDU6SXNzdWUxNDM0OTcyMjE=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWUxNDM0OTcyMjE=.Body:
There are clearly two scenarios for adding nodes and edges to a graph: operations that must be validated and possibly complemented (i.e., operations done by the user through the GUI), and operations that are simply reconstruction of a graph for stored versions (e.g., from persisted graphs or from copy-pasting operations). Currently the two modes are tangled, but the code would be cleaner if they were separated.
Try to see if as part of this we can solve the problem of ObjectGraph nodes getting pasted/undeleted back in alphabetical order, independent of the initial ordering (which should be preserved).

MDU6SXNzdWUzMjY4NzQ3MzA=.Title: Eliminate Aliasing on Canvas Rendering
MDU6SXNzdWUzMjY4NzQ3MzA=.Author: prmr

It's possible to draw crisp strokes. 

See: http://dlsc.com/2014/04/10/javafx-tip-2-sharp-drawing-with-canvas-api/
https://stackoverflow.com/questions/11886230/how-to-draw-a-crisp-opaque-hairline-in-javafx-2-2

MDU6SXNzdWUzNTI2OTM4MDA=.Title: Centering of Object diagram name incorrect with long value field.
MDU6SXNzdWUzNTI2OTM4MDA=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWUzNTI2OTM4MDA=.Body:
In Release 2.1 with Java 1.8.0_171 on Windows 10.

The name of objects in Object diagram does not center properly when the value of one of more field names is too long.

## Steps to reproduce

1. Create an new Object diagram
2. Create an new object node in the diagram
3. Name the node "Foo"
4. Add a field to the object node.
5. Change the value of the `value` properties of the field node to `XXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX`

The name of the node will be justified to the left instead of being centered.

## Root Cause

In `ObjectNodeView.draw`, the bounds of the top rectangle and the entire object don't have the same width.

MDU6SXNzdWUxMTg4MzY5NjQ=.Title: Creating an ImplicitParameterNode on an empty Diagram should include a CallNode

MDU6SXNzdWU1NDUxODU4NzQ=.Title: Edge bounds don't include the label in Sequence Diagram
MDU6SXNzdWU1NDUxODU4NzQ=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU1NDUxODU4NzQ=.Body:
**Description**
The edge bounds in sequence diagrams don't include the bounds for the label. As a result, when an edge's label extend past the actual edge, the window does not scroll when the label hits the right sides of the view pane, and the label is clipped when the diagram is exported.

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Create a new sequence diagram 
2. Create a self-edge and give it a long name
3. copy to clipboard, past in an external application
4. See edge clipped

**Screenshots**
![test](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6033492/71752129-efdec380-2e4b-11ea-9829-f7ec3a007d24.png)

**Configuration**
 - JetUML Release: 2.4
 - OS + version: Windows 10 (applies to all)
 - Java version: 8 (applies to all)
 
**Additional information**
The fix is to add the label's bound in `CallEdgeViewer.getBounds`.

MDU6SXNzdWUzODUwNTM4ODM=.Title: Unable to paste any subgraph containing a PointNode
MDU6SXNzdWUzODUwNTM4ODM=.Author: kkutschera
MDU6SXNzdWUzODUwNTM4ODM=.Body:
**Description**
Unable to paste any subgraph containing a PointNode.

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Open any new diagram
2. Create a Note and attach a NoteEdge
3. Select the Note and attached NoteEdge
4. Copy and paste -> selection is not pasted

**Suspected Cause**
In the Clipboard, validNodeFor() is called to check if a paste is valid. Unfortunately, this only checks to see if the Node is contained in the diagram's node prototypes which PointNode is not included in for any diagram type.

Comments.Body: Good catch. We could just return `true` for `PointNode` as the first statement. This oversight points to the need for better documentation and tests for `validPaste`
Comments.Author: prmr

MDU6SXNzdWU1NTUxMjM2NjI=.Title: Moving parent node through child goes beyond Canvas
MDU6SXNzdWU1NTUxMjM2NjI=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU1NTUxMjM2NjI=.Body:
**Description**
When moving selected nodes, the bounds checks are done on the selected nodes. However, in some cases, such as when a node is in a package node in a class diagram, moving the child node results in the parent node being repositioned. In such cases, it is possible to bring the bounds of the outer node beyond the canvas.

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Create a new class diagram
2. Create a package node
3. Create a class node that is a child of the package node (by clicking inside the package node)
4. Select the class node and move it completely to the left (or top, right, bottom) of the canvas.

The parent node goes off the canvas.

**Screenshots**
![jetuml](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6033492/73125962-f4922580-3f7a-11ea-8168-dd4f609a9a15.png)

**Configuration**
 - JetUML Release: 2.5
 - OS + version: All versions
 - Java version: All versions 
 
**Additional information**
 Note that package nodes can nest more than one level deep.

Create new class diagram. 2. Create a package node and a class node beside the package. 3. Create a class node inside the package node. MDU6SXNzdWU4NzI1NDk5Nzk=.Title: Use enums to list property names
MDU6SXNzdWU4NzI1NDk5Nzk=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU4NzI1NDk5Nzk=.Body:
The name of a `Property` in the `Properties` mini-framework is specified using a `String`, which is also used to serialize the property. This is brittle, and makes it hard to track property names through the code. We can use an enum for tighter dependency checking.

For some dependency (e.g., `Generalization Type`), the name of the property is not externalized, so the tool actually uses the internal name of the property in the UI. This should be fixed.

MDU6SXNzdWU5NTA0MzI2OQ==.Title: Simplify the parent node semantics
MDU6SXNzdWU5NTA0MzI2OQ==.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU5NTA0MzI2OQ==.Body:
Parent nodes should act as "master" for their children, with the following consequences:
- It should be impossible to select both a child and its parent. Selecting a parent automatically applies all operations (e.g., clone, translate) to the children.
- The graph object should only track root nodes (nodes with a `null` parent), as all other nodes can be tracked through their parent.
- Drawing children nodes should be done recursively through their parent
- Cloning children nodes should be done recursively through their parent
- Translating children nodes should be done recursively through their parent
- Copying a node and pasting it should paste it back to its original parent, the only catch with that is that for package nodes, it would be impossible to cut a node from inside the package and copy it outside. For this we would need some sort of 'detach' operation, or take into account the position of the mouse when the command is issued.

@EJBQ  @prmr The TabbedView is starting to come along! I invite you both to check it out and play with it a bit. I have yet to look into adding a splash screen (so on launch there is nothing on display, you have to add a tab to start interacting with JetUML). If there are any important features that I have not ported over yet, please let me know and I will look into them.

Some important changes:
-To close an open tab, you can either hit CTRL-W or go File->Close to close the current tab. If the GraphPanel in the current tab has been modified, it will launch a YES/NO OptionPane to confirm that you want to close the tab
-The Window menu of JetUML has been ripped out (as you don't need to resize windows or close them anymore).

For now, I am treating tabs as JInternalFrames (so either GrameFrames or an eventual WelcomeFrame splash tab as we discussed last week). If either of you feel that I should change this to a different Swing component (JPanel or something), please let me know and I can change this quickly. Thanks!

MDU6SXNzdWUxMTM5NTI3NzE=.Title: Simplify the Edge Hierarchy
MDU6SXNzdWUxMTM5NTI3NzE=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWUxMTM5NTI3NzE=.Body:
- `ShapedEdge` can be pulled up into `AbstractEdge`, and the `getShape` method should be made protected for a true implementation of the Template Method Design Pattern.
- The start, middle, and end label properties can be removed from the `SegmentedEdge` because they are not used for `CallEdge` and `ReturnEdge`.
- `getShape` can be used uniformly to draw the edge
- `ClassRelationshipEdge` can be split into multiple types and the visual properties encoded as class-instance fields. The segmentation algorithms should be implemented as strategies.

and so on.

MDU6SXNzdWUzMzc1MDI3OTE=.Title: Model-View-Controller Refactoring
MDU6SXNzdWUzMzc1MDI3OTE=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWUzMzc1MDI3OTE=.Body:
Split the diagram construction logic into a different class so that we have three class hierarchies to address the three main concerns with diagrams:
* `Diagram`: Stores the logical structure of the diagram only. Indepedent of any geometry or GUI
* `DiagramBuilder`: Business rules to create diagram from various interactions with a Canvas. 
* `DiagramViewer`: Responsible for computing the geometry of the diagram (through delegation to nodes)

MDU6SXNzdWU2MjMzOTE3Njc=.Title: Sequence Diagram deletion does not preserve control-flow constraints
MDU6SXNzdWU2MjMzOTE3Njc=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU2MjMzOTE3Njc=.Body: **Description** Sequence diagram should only allow the construction of sequence that represent a valid control flow rooted at a single entry point. This constraint is enforced by the `SequenceDiagramBuilder` for adding to a diagram, but not when deleting nodes and edges. Deleting nodes and edges can currently result in an invalid diagram. Additional auto-deletions need to be added to ensure the downstream calls are also deleted. **Steps to reproduce** 1. Create new Sequence diagram 2. Create a diagram like this: 3. Delete the call edge from N1 to N2 **Configuration** - JetUML Release: 2.6 - OS + version: All - Java version: All

MDU6SXNzdWU0NjgyMTMwNDc=.Title: Implement edge viewing through stateless strategies
MDU6SXNzdWU0NjgyMTMwNDc=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU0NjgyMTMwNDc=.Body:
Edge view calculations is currently done using a wrapper `EdgeView` object that can be obtained from the `edge` instance being wrapped. Redesign this code so that the edge viewing is done using a registry of stateless edge viewer strategies. The anticipated design benefits are:

* It will be possible to reuse viewer objects instead of having replicates with identical behavior for each each of the same type;
* It will facilitate achieving a complete separation between the `Edge` clas hierarchy and the viewing code, so as to completely sever the dependency to JavaFX classes from the `Edge hierarchy`.
* It will simplify the code, by avoiding complex state management for edges which currently require a reference to their diagram. By passing the diagram to the strategy, it will be possible to get rid of this dependency.

MDU6SXNzdWU2MzE3OTMzNjk=.Title: Refactor the handling of the tool prototypes
MDU6SXNzdWU2MzE3OTMzNjk=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU2MzE3OTMzNjk=.Body:
Improve on the following two design weaknesses:
1. The nodes and edges, as prototypes of creating new diagram elements, are unnecessarily split up: they could be treated polymorphically as `DiagramElements` in a lot of the code, thus simplifying it.
2. The tooltip for the node and edge prototypes are indexed in the properties files using integers that represent their sequence, which is brittle. Instead, this key should be stored in an object.

MDU6SXNzdWU1MzU4MzU2NTQ=.Title: Split ClassNode from Interface Node
MDU6SXNzdWU1MzU4MzU2NTQ=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU1MzU4MzU2NTQ=.Body:
In the current design `ClassNode` extends `InterfaceNode`, which is a design weakness because there is no "is-a" relationship between them. They should be sibling subtypes of a common abstract `TypeNode`.

As a result of this patch the design of the nodes has been improved: they have been made slightly more compact when there are methods and interfaces.

MDU6SXNzdWU0NTc1OTYzODA=.Title: Assertion violation in Viewport projection
MDU6SXNzdWU0NTc1OTYzODA=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU0NTc1OTYzODA=.Body:
**Description**
Dragging an node to the right or bottom of the canvas generates an `AssertionError`. Resizing the window afterwards keeps generating the error.

**Steps to reproduce**

1. Create a new diagram
2. Create a new node
3. Drag the node to the right edge of the canvas, then keep dragging.

An assertion error will be displayed on the console:

```
Exception in thread "JavaFX Application Thread" java.lang.AssertionError
	at ca.mcgill.cs.jetuml.gui.ViewportProjection.<init>(ViewportProjection.java:53)
	at ca.mcgill.cs.jetuml.gui.DiagramTab.getViewportProjection(DiagramTab.java:305)
	at ca.mcgill.cs.jetuml.gui.DiagramTab.interactionTo(DiagramTab.java:296)
	at ca.mcgill.cs.jetuml.gui.DiagramCanvasController.mouseDragged(DiagramCanvasController.java:513)
	at com.sun.javafx.event.CompositeEventHandler.dispatchBubblingEvent(CompositeEventHandler.java:86)
```

**Configuration**
 - JetUML Release: 2.3 Commit 97690c
 - OS + version: Windows 10
 - Java version: 8u211

**Diagnostic**

This problem only seems to occur when the scrollbars are not enabled. Some some reasons, when the scrollbars are not visible, the viewport is 10 pixels larger than the canvas in both directions.

MDU6SXNzdWU5NzU3MzQ1MTM=.Title: Do not delete implicit parameter when constructor call is deleted
MDU6SXNzdWU5NzU3MzQ1MTM=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU5NzU3MzQ1MTM=.Body:
In a sequence diagram, if we have a constructor call (see below) and delete the call, the implicit parameter also gets deleted. To be consistent with the deletion behavior for other edges, we should not delete the node.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6033492/130260803-240f8c11-5c51-4c90-942c-5c0b3328c032.png)

MDU6SXNzdWU2Mjc3NzM2Njc=.Title: Additional Edge Constraints
MDU6SXNzdWU2Mjc3NzM2Njc=.Author: prmr
MDU6SXNzdWU2Mjc3NzM2Njc=.Body:
It should not be possible to create circular generalization cycles. This constraint will be limited to two-node cycles until we have a more detailed feedback mechanism. This constraint will not be migrated, so loading old diagrams can violate it, but it does not impact the diagram viewing.

The same should apply to aggregation and association edges.

It should also not be possible to have *both* an aggregation and an association edge between two node, since aggregation is a form of association.

I_kwDOAblrlc5f_AxX.Title: Addition of Inner Class Relationships in Class Diagrams
I_kwDOAblrlc5f_AxX.Author: YovelB
I_kwDOAblrlc5f_AxX.Body:
### Detailed Description
<!--- Provide a detailed description of the change or addition you are proposing -->
Currently UML Class Diagrams don't have a proper way to represent Inner classes. Because, UML needs to represent all languages.

Therefore, I suggest a basic relationship that can represents at least two types of inner classes (basic and nested).
The addition can be made more subtle by adding it to "edit" toolbar. While, not overpopulating the right side tools.
A relationship can be made of a crossed circle with an arrow pointing towards the inner class.

**for example:**
![template](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/25065136/222984129-796e3498-56a5-4470-84e6-8e2e561e2b21.gif)

### Context
<!--- Why is this change important to you? How would you use it? -->
<!--- How can it benefit other users? -->
This change is important because it can highlight and explain better UML models. I like using nested classes and would like to represent it in my models.
Adding another relationships adds more complexity and understanding that needs to be explain to make clear models.
Other Java modelers can benefit from it by switching from other UML designers to JetUML. Because it is focused on the Java language features.

Comments.Body: An interesting idea, but too specialized a scenario for the general sketching mission of the tool.
Comments.Author: prmr

MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0Mzg1ODYwMjE5.Title: #269 Create Constructor Call Support in Sequence Diagram
MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0Mzg1ODYwMjE5.Body:
**Summary of Changes**

- Create a new type of edge `ConstructorEdge` which is inherited from the CallEdge.
- Use the `CallEdgeViewer` for drawing the `ConstructorEdge`.
- Modify the edge constraints to allow constructor call.

**Achieved Performance**

- A new type of edge to better distinguish the constructor call.
- Can create constructor call when the call edge lands on the `ImplicitParameterNode`.

**Concerns**

- The label of the `ConstructorEdge` is set to  "create" with bracket symbols by default. However, since we are inheriting directly from the CallEdge, which is a subclass of `SingleLabelEdge`, we can still change the label after creation.
MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0Mzg1ODYwMjE5.Author: cc-21

- Adjusted `StringViewer2` to be more concise and work with all node types
- Updated multiple tests including those needing the JavaFX toolkit initialized
- Note: `ClassDiagramGraph2*` are almost direct copies of `ClassDiagramGraph*`

MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0MTc1MTI4OTMz.Title: #231 Integrate Use Case Diagram that is Migrated to JavaFX
MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0MTc1MTI4OTMz.Body:
Summary of Changes:
- Created Use Case Diagram 2 which is a version of Use Case Diagram that has been completely migrated to JavaFX which includes all its graphics
- To integrate the fully migrated `UseCaseDiagramGraph2` with the other diagrams, a second version of multiple classes has been created and a few key classes decide which version to use
- `GraphPanel2` extends `Canvas` which has been modified to make it resizable. Since the canvas needs a maximum size to work properly it has been set to twice the user’s screen size. Any larger and the user will notice more lag and any less and the zoom functionality will cease completely
- The zoom functionality was reduced to a single zoom in and a single zoom out otherwise the canvas will reach its limits within the viewing area
- In Export to Image, work arounds were necessary to properly create JPG, JPEG, and BMP. WBMP is no longer supported for the migrated Use Case Diagram
- `GraphicsContext` of the canvas doesn’t have a `draw(Shape)` method so in `AbstractNodeView` `getShape()` was replaced with `fillShape()`
- When drawing paths in `GraphicsContent` any changes to the look (ie. color, line style, etc.) must be set between `beginPath()` and `fill()`/`stroke()` so `completeDrawPath()` has been added to `AbstractEdgeView`

Outline of Modified Classes:
- migrated versions of files that are **nearly identical** to originals (often just swapping versions of classes): `Clipboard2`, `GraphModificationListener2`, `MoveTracker2`, `*Command2`, `UseCaseDiagramGraph2`, `Conversions2`, `Graph2`, `GraphFrame2`, `AbstractContext2`, `DeserializationContext2`, `JsonDecoder2`, `JsonEncoder2`, `PersistenceServices2`, `SerializationContext2`, `EdgeView2`, `SegmentationStyle2`, `SegementationStyleFactory2`, `NodeView2`
- modified classes to allow **integration**: `*Edge`, `*Node`, `EditorFrame`, `ArrowHead`, `LineStyle`
- classes with **large changes** (generally the drawing methods): `EditorFrame`, `GraphPanel2`, `ToolBar2` (removed all Swing components), `ArrowHead2`, `Grid2`, `StringViewer2`, `AbstractEdgeView2`, `*EdgeView2`, `AbstractNodeView2`, `*NodeView2`
- test classes: any modifications are either an addition to initialize JavaFX toolkit and environment or small fixes to accommodate new code
- Note: `*View2` files with `//TODO: TO BE COMPLETED` at the top are incomplete and will be completed once their corresponding diagram is migrated to JavaFX
MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0MTc1MTI4OTMz.Author: kkutschera

PR_kwDOAblrlc4w96Lr.Title: bug fix issue #445
PR_kwDOAblrlc4w96Lr.Body: Took the path of least resistance to fix this bug. Yet, this fix is somewhat incomplete. In DiagramCanvasController::moveSelection(Point) it would be better to consider the case where dragging a nested node would not affect one or more of the boundaries of the enclosing node (e.g having 2 nested nodes in a package node and dragging only one of them). In this case, we would not have to check the enclosing boundary for being out of canvas range. Incidentally, this creates an upper bound on the moving speed of a node when the enclosing boundary is close to the canvas edge.
PR_kwDOAblrlc4w96Lr.Author: lberardelli

Before, the text was indiscriminately set to center horizontally. MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0NDM3NDg3Nzkw.Title: #363 Add auto-selection algorithm to avoid redundant node creation
MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0NDM3NDg3Nzkw.Body:
**Summary of Changes**

- Modify the method in the `DiagramCanvasController` class to handle the auto-selection.

**Achieved Performance**

- If the user clicks on the canvas and the click is within an object's bound, then automatically activate the Selection Tool in the toolbar and select the element clicked.
- Auto-selection is implemented only for nodes other than `ImplicitParameterNode`s and `FieldNode`s since we may actually want to add a child node or a field to the node under the click.

**Concerns**

- Auto-selection is disabled for edges since we may want to detect the end node of the edge instead of selecting the node.
MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0NDM3NDg3Nzkw.Author: cc-21

PR_kwDOAblrlc4uHEAs.Title: Issue375 : Add a NodeStorage, unit tests, and a performance test.
PR_kwDOAblrlc4uHEAs.Body:
### Add NodeStorage
As described in the commit message, a NodeStorage was added to each NodeViewer to improve the performance of DiagramViewer.draw(diagram). For each call to NodeViewer.getBounds(node), if the NodeStorage is deactivated, then the bounds will be computed as returned. If the NodeStorage is activated, then it will first check whether the bounds have already been computed, and if so, it will return them directly. Otherwise, it will compute the bounds to be returned and store them for future reference. As soon as the NodeStorage is deactivated, it is cleared.

I made use of the Template Method design pattern in AbstractNodeViewer by defining the abstract method internalGetBounds(Node) which returns a Rectangle. A method reference to internalGetBounds(Node) is passed to the NodeStorage to indicate how to compute the bounds for each of the different NodeViewers. 

This NodeStorage is activated at the beginning of the method DiagramViewer.draw(diagram) and is deactivated/cleared at the end of the same method. 

### Add a performance test
The performance test is defined in the class TestPerformance, in a main method. Originally, it could be executed from the test folder if the package was mentioned in module-info.java. However, this seems to have changed. I have only been able to execute the main method from the src folder (no longer needs to be mentioned in module-info.java). If there is a better alternative, we can move the file elsewhere. 

A performance test was added to be able to witness the benefits of adding a NodeStorage. The test loads a class diagram into memory from an external file and records the execution time of DiagramViewer.draw(diagram). To increase the accuracy of the reported duration, the average duration of ten calls to _draw_ is reported, omitting the initial outlier. The diagram under observation has 11 nodes. There are a total of 1342 calls to NodeStorage.getBounds(Node, Function<Node, Rectangle>), of which 11 are cache misses and 1331 are cache hits. The duration reported by the performance test dropped from approximately 26ms to 16ms with the NodeStorage implementation.
PR_kwDOAblrlc4uHEAs.Author: delphinepilon

**Summary of changes:**

`ConstraintSet` is now stored as a `private static final` field in each
diagram builder. This reduces object creation by eliminating the need to
create and merge new `ConstraintSet` objects each time `canAdd` is
called. I removed `merge` method in `ConstraintSet` and `getAdditionalEdgeConstraints` method in `DiagramBuilder`  
since they are no longer needed in the new design. `getAdditionalEdgeConstraints` allowed `canAdd` to gather all necessary constraints but at the cost of excessive object creation. 

Now, `canAdd` can get all constraints for each diagram type with the method `getEdgeConstraints()`. The `ConstraintSet` field can be accessed by this method which simply returns the set of constraints. I
see no encapsulation issue in doing so since there is no method available to modify contents of the `ConstraintSet` to be returned. 

 `Constraint.satisfied()` now takes in parameters in order to check the
conditions as such `satisfied(Edge pEdge, Node pStart, Node pEnd, Point pStartPoint, Point pEndPoint, Diagram pDiagramBuilder)`. This design decision was agreed upon to have a clean way to check for constraints without needing an object creation heavy method like `getAdditionalEdgeConstraints`.

Unit tests specific to these changes were also refactored.
MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0NTc4OTM5ODUy.Author: Farihatanjin

PR_kwDOAblrlc5vj2tJ.Title: [#547] Fix Connection Point of Edges and Nodes in State Diagrams
PR_kwDOAblrlc5vj2tJ.Body:
### Issue
Edges in state diagrams connecting to the corner SW and SE points of rounded rectangles are positioned incorrectly. This can happen at either endpoints of the edge, as shown below.
![image](https://github.com/prmr/JetUML/assets/113862811/42d24e4d-99e3-41bf-8940-d0b5c604c239)

### Location of Error
The error was in `GeomUtils#intersectRectangle`, which is called by `GeomUtils#intersectRoundedRectangle` to calculate the precise connection point on the rounded portions of the rounded rectangle.

### What was Wrong
The problem occurs when the edge is positioned on the exact corner point in the SW and SE direction.
The if conditions in `intersectRectangle` uses the `ifBetween(start, end)` method to check whether a direction is between start and end. The start and end value themselves are exclusive. For instance, 60° would not be between 60° (start) and 90° (end). (The documentation, which says that the starting direction is inclusive, may have been the reason for the oversight which led to this miscalculation).

So, by the logic of the if/else-if/else branch in `intersectRectangle`, if a user positions two nodes such that the edge is pointing in the exact direction of `diagonalSE`, the flow of execution would be delegated to the `else` branch, despite the fact that it should be handled by one of the appropriate `if/else if` branch, which in this case could be `else if( pDirection.isBetween(diagonalNE, diagonalSE) )`. Interestingly, this did not impact the NW and NE corners because these two cases can be handled appropriately by the `else` branch in any case.

### Fix
By incrementing the range of the end values by 1 to include corner directions, we can ensure that the calculation is handled by the correct branch of if statements, and thus compute the offset for the connection points between node and edge correctly. To continue the example above, to fix the connection point for the SE corner, we can include the SE corner direction with the following code: `else if( pDirection.isBetween(diagonalNE, diagonalSE.rotatedBy(1)) )`
PR_kwDOAblrlc5vj2tJ.Author: jkim7x
