MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0NjI5NzcyNjIz.Title: #426 font resizing
MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0NjI5NzcyNjIz.Body:
Summary of Changes
- Add `fontSize` as an `IntegerPreference` in `UserPreferences.java`
- Made `IntegerPreference`s observable by adding an `IntegerPreferenceChangeHandler` interface as well as registration (`addIntegerPreferenceChangeHandler`) and deregistration (`removeIntegerPreferenceChangeHandler`) methods.
- Create class `FontSizeDialog` which provides a modal interface for modifying and resetting the font size. To access, `EditorFrame` adds a slot under the "View" section which upon clicking, instantiates an instance of `FontSizeDialog`.
- Add values to `JetUML.properties` which are used to populate `FontSizeDialog` and `EditorFrame`.
- Add a `wrapString` method in `StringViewer` which takes in a string and character length and returns a copy of the string with newlines interleaved so that each line is less than the specified character length
- Add a private inner-class `CanvasFont` to `StringViewer` which observes `IntegerPreference`s (specifically font size) and whose API (`getDimension`, `fontSize`, and `drawString`) all execute with respect to the current font size
- Update `StateTransitionEdgeViewer` so that it (1) uses `StringViewer` to draw its labels (2) shortens labels by using `wrapString` instead of modifying the font size and (3) the positioning of labels which occurred in function of the angle between the start and end nodes only apply at certain angles (to avoid over-correction when the font size is large)
- Update `AbstractPackageNodeViewer` and `PackageNodeViewer` so that the name of packages resizes vertically to accommodate large fonts
- Update `CallEdgeNodeViewer` to position call nodes to accommodate possible overlapping labels at sufficiently large fonts
- Replace constant-value label shifts with shifts calculated with respect to node label height (`CallEdgeViewer`, `LabeledStraightEdgeViewer`
- Changed precomputed values regarding label dimensions to be computed at each repaint (`FieldNodeViewer`)
- Add tests for `wrapString` and `StringViewer` operations at different font sizes (Note: These will probably fail on other OSes)
- Update existing, failing tests (Note: Some will probably fail on other OSes)
MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0NjI5NzcyNjIz.Author: yannsartori

MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0NTU1OTM5Mzgw.Title: #402 Save and restore text alignment properties in drawString
MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0NTU1OTM5Mzgw.Body:
**Summary of the changes:**

The `drawString` method gets called multiple times for the same label before it gets drawn. These calls did not always have the same old alignment values, ranging from center,center to top,left. This change makes it always center, center, regardless of the previous state.
MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0NTU1OTM5Mzgw.Author: yannsartori

Comments.Body:
Can you explain the solution a bit more and perhaps clarify the title? The following questions remains:
*  Why is `drawString` called multiple times for a single label?
* Why is the alignment forced to be top-left when some strings should be left-justified, some right justified?
* The code changes the alignment to center. What's the deal with top left?
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body:
> Can you explain the solution a bit more and perhaps clarify the title? The following questions remains:
> 
> * Why is `drawString` called multiple times for a single label?
> * Why is the alignment forced to be top-left when some strings should be left-justified, some right justified?
> * The code changes the alignment to center. What's the deal with top left?

Sure.
1. It’s actually the entire diagram which gets painted multiple times (which makes the edges, and hence, the labels, get drawn multiple times). From the call stack, if you switch selection, it gets called 1. By DiagramCanvas.selectionModelChange() 2. The mousePressed handler 3. the DiagramCanvasControler.alignMoveToGrid call and 4. the releaseMove event handler. 
 2.  The way it was drawn, it would draw the nodes first and then the edges (from DiagramViewer.draw()). The issue is when it draws the nodes, it doesn’t save the state and restore it after the drawing, as it does for the edges. So prior to these changes, the edges would get the state right after drawing the node. A node is drawn first by title (which sets the alignment to center, center), then its attributes, if it has any (which sets its alignment to top, left). Furthermore, it draws the selected node first. So if you select a node with attributes it draws it, then the other node with none, setting the state to center center. Then, the start and end labels have this alignment. In contrast, if you select a node with no attributes, it draws it, then the other node (which has attributes), setting the state to top left. It would probably be less error prone for other things as well if the graphics’ state was saved and restored as well. Should I include that in this PR?
 3. I actually changed it slightly. Originally, I just set it that way because I thought it looked the nicest (otherwise there are huge spaces, it clips with the node/edge, and other unpleasant stuff). When I was going back to justify this, I realized that the start, center, and end label would actually benefit from having their own, custom alignments, so I made these as parameters to the function. Is this fine?
Comments.Author: yannsartori
prmr

PR_kwDOAblrlc4w4ybJ.Title: #437 Fix Downstream ImplicitParameterNode removal when a ConstructorEdge removed
PR_kwDOAblrlc4w4ybJ.Body:
SequenceDiagramBuilder.getCoRemovals() now includes line which checks whether the explicit parameter is an object of type ConstructorEdge, and if so, removes downstream objects of type ImplicitParameterNode. This ensures that when a constructor edge is removed, its downstream implicit parameter nodes are not also removed, in order to be consistent with the effects of deleting call edges. A unit test for this specific change was also produced. 

Below are images showing the fix:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/90351737/149239257-74f99f70-0003-40b5-9512-bcd4a9d33b83.png)

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/90351737/149239272-ab76294f-2247-4fa8-9490-bff05e1f781f.png)
PR_kwDOAblrlc4w4ybJ.Author: maddy0218

MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0MzY0MzAxMDQ4.Title: #142 Reorder the selected elements to be drawn on top
MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0MzY0MzAxMDQ4.Body:
**Summary of Changes:**

Add a _rootNodesContain(Node): boolean_ method in the Diagram class so that one could easily check if a node is a root node of the Diagram. Add the reordering algorithm/method in the DiagramCanvasController class by iterating through the selected elements and reorder each element recursively.

**Achieved Performance:**
1. Whichever root node selected would be on top of all other root nodes of the Diagram.

2. Nested node: a. The selected child node would be on top of all the other children of the same parent node. b. The parent of the selected child node would be on top of all the other nodes within the same depth level as it is. If the parent is also a child node, do this recursively (until reaching the root nodes of the Diagram/ the node itself does not have a parent).

**Design Concerns:**

1. The _rootNodesContain(Node): boolean_ method could be omitted and one could iterate the root nodes of the Diagram to check if the given node is a root or not. Since the diagram is only responsible for handling its root nodes and leaves the children nodes to their parent node to handle, I feel that such a checking method might be useful if we want to add more complex operations dealing with the parent nodes (e.g. package nodes).

2. I separate the reordering method _reorderSelectedNode(Node): void_ from the _handleSelection_ method so that it could be recursively called. I choose the recursive design instead of iterative to have better readability of the code.

3. The reordering is dealing with nodes only. The position order of the edge during the movement/selection does not really impede the visual display. Could look for future improvements.
MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0MzY0MzAxMDQ4.Author: cc-21

PR_kwDOAblrlc5nA3CB.Title: StringRenderer Redesign
PR_kwDOAblrlc5nA3CB.Body:
Class `StringRenderer` provides an interface for subsystems to render strings to their particular needs for different font styles and text alignments. 

The redesign cleaned up class `StringRenderer` to provide a minimal interface to work with. This involved moving the static inner class `CanvasFont` of `StringRenderer` to a class of its own.

MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0MjMzMDc4MDI0.Title: #279 - Add staggering when pasting
MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0MjMzMDc4MDI0.Body:
Adds staggering when subgraphs are pasted multiple times. Subgraphs are re-positioned to (x,x) from (0,0) in increments of GRID_SIZE if subgraph would overlap with existing subgraph.

Notes:
- I originally anticipated only a need to check if there was an existing node at position (x,x) to prevent staggering. Unfortunately that does not suffice. If we copy and paste a subgraph that does not have a node in the top-left corner of the selection (say pasting a subgraph with a node in the top-right corner and in the bottom-left corner of the selection) solely looking to see if there is a node at position (x,x) will result in overlapping on this subgraph. Thus, my algorithm checks to see if the position of the subgraph nodes are the same as nodes currently in the graph. If this is the case, the subgraph nodes are shifted.

- Although there is no noticeable lag in normal use cases of JetUML, when pasting large subgraphs (tens to hundreds of nodes) multiple times does result in a noticeable lag.


Finally, let me know if you have any suggestions on improvements or if you can think of better ways of implementing staggering.
MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0MjMzMDc4MDI0.Author: kkutschera

Comments.Body:
Three comments:
1. About your first note, instead of using the position, can't we make the same reasoning by computing the bounds of the set of pasted elements? E.g., with `Rectangle.add(Rectangle)`? 
2. The edges don't draw in the right order. This is not really a regression, but it makes it more apparent. I wonder if there's an easy way to address this.
3. Design wise, I'm a bit weary of adding complexity (including a parameter) to the `Builder` methods. This is a very complex part of the code. How about creating an additional move operation as part of the pasting code?
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body:
To address your comments:
1) I can't think of a good way to use bounds. It is easy to compute the bounds of the pasted elements, but there isn't a good way to compare the bounds of the pasted elements with the elements currently on the screen. Noting that the bounds of the pasted elements are contained in the bounds of the current elements doesn't provide any useful definitive information. (ex. having two current nodes, one in the top right, another in the bottom left, would result in the current bounds being the entire canvas.) For it to be of any value, we'd need to see if there is a subset of current elements with the same bounds as the bounds of the pasted elements which would be more complex than by doing it using positions. Let me know if you can think of another way to use bounds instead since it would be nicer to use existing methods rather than creating new position methods.
2) I looked into the edge ordering. I didn't find a cause, so I'm not sure if there is an easy way to correct it.
3) I've removed the changes to the Builder class and created a shift method to be used by the paste method.

Question:
Is there a reason we want the subgraphs to be pasted in the corner rather than shifted from its current position? I can double-check, but I think simply removing updatePosition() from copying in the Clipboard would allow pasting shifted from its current position instead of the corner.
Comments.Author: kkutschera

Comments.Body:
1. You're right, computing the bounds would require obtaining a `View` object, which is starting to become cumbersome.
2. The issue with the edges is simply that `DiagramView.draw` draws all the nodes, then all the edges. It's interesting that this is only really an issue with pasting. Ultimately we will only be able to solve this once we finally add a Z dimension to the tool. Soon, but not for this PR. So, let's ignore.
3. Great.

Question: No real reason actually. Come to think of it, it might be better.
Comments.Author: prmr

Comments.Body:
- I've added the logic to stagger the pasting from the subgraph's original position
- Additionally, I've ensured that the pasted elements are automatically selected once they are pasted. Before merging this branch I want to look into another way of detecting overlap using the selection bounds
Comments.Author: kkutschera

Comments.Body: I've reworked the staggering to use bounds rather than positions. Overall, I think it is better. But, I had to implement a special case for sequence diagrams since the exclusion of activation bars when copying messes with the bounds. So for sequence diagrams I used the condition that the bounds have to be the same _excluding the height_ for it to be overlapped and thus needs to be staggered.
Comments.Author: kkutschera

Comments.Body: I definitely agree that having diagram-type dependent logic in the controller is awkward. Moving it to DiagramView is much cleaner
Comments.Author: kkutschera

MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0NTcyNjczMjc4.Title: 413 refactor the string viewer
MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0NTcyNjczMjc4.Body:
Summary of the changes:

- Made a class that represents the font metrics for a particular font. It can take in a string, and then output the dimensions of the string, taking into account any potential leading or ascent that may arise. This dimension is tight with respect to these values.
- Changed classes (`StringViewer` and `AbstractEdgeViewer`) that rely on text dimensions directly to utilize this class, rather than doing the calculations themselves.
- Fix tests that were made broken by the change in how precisely the dimensions are determined (notably, it takes into account any _potential_ ascent or descent, as opposed to the old version which only accounted for it if it was there, and making the bounds tighter
- Added a test for the new class that tests both that the dimension calculated is correct and that there is no potential state saving upon testing different strings.
MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0NTcyNjczMjc4.Author: yannsartori

PR_kwDOAblrlc5dl-I7.Title: Notification-based feedback system graphical component
PR_kwDOAblrlc5dl-I7.Body:
This pull request follows #508, by consisting of the graphical part of the notification-based feedback system.

It adds a new interface, `Notification`, and two new classes, `NotificationHandler` and `ToastNotification`.
A single test file effectively test both classes (they work together).

The `NotificationHandler` is a _Singleton_ whose `spawn()` method can be called from anywhere to display a Toast notification on the screen. A `ToastNotification` object can be even more customized by using the detailed constructor of the class.

Every class implementing the `Notification` interface can be used with the `NotificationHandler`.
PR_kwDOAblrlc5dl-I7.Author: ArthusWQZ

PR_kwDOAblrlc4w914v.Body: Took the path of least resistance to fix this bug. Yet, this fix is somewhat incomplete. In DiagramCanvasController::moveSelection(Point) it would be better to consider the case where dragging a nested node would not affect one or more of the boundaries of the enclosing node (e.g having 2 nested nodes in a package node and dragging only one of them). In this case, we would not have to check the enclosing boundary for being out of canvas range. Incidentally, this creates an upper bound on the moving speed of a node when the enclosing boundary is close to the canvas edge.

MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0NTA1NjM1NzY5.Title: [#328] Support zooming the diagram
MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0NTA1NjM1NzY5.Body:
# Description
- Zooming in/out uses an exponential scale, with a base of 1.25
- Each tab maintains its own independent zoom level
- Allows user to reset zoom using `ctrl + 0`.
  - This required adding a filter on the toolbar selection hotkeys

# Limitations
Although #328 mentions using `ctrl + '+'` to zoom in, I bound it to `ctrl + '='` as I believe this shortcut is much more intuitive. The perfect solution would bind both `ctrl + '+'` and `ctrl + '='` to this function though unfortunately that is not easily achieved using `MenuItem`s.

Icons for the new menu items are missing.

PR_kwDOAblrlc50UpBw.Title: [#553] Scroll Bar Fix
PR_kwDOAblrlc50UpBw.Body:
Scroll bars unnecessarily appear on the `TextArea` in the properties dialog of a node, if the user has their monitor display setting at a higher scale (greater than 100% on Windows).

The scroll bars appear because the dimension of the `TextArea` is larger than the space that is allocated by its `ScrollPane` parent. This mismatch seems to occur due to JavaFX not considering the effect scale has on dimension computation for different components.
PR_kwDOAblrlc50UpBw.Author: jkim7x

PR_kwDOAblrlc4x-xeJ.Body: This addresses the issues brought in in #445 . However, we could now open a separate bug ticket for the following: on a "MouseDragged" event, we handle the movement of the selection in DiagramCanvasController::moveSelection. In the case where the entire selection bounds is close the wall of the canvas, we obtain an upper bound on the tracking speed due to the logic in DiagramCanvasController::moveSelection. This PR features a new Font Dialog for users to change the font and size to their preferred setting. This involved:

Also, with the addition of users being able to select fonts, the font name needed to be set and restored for a consistent testing environment.

MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0NTU1OTAxNjEw.Title: #400 Verify and update failing tests
MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0NTU1OTAxNjEw.Body:
Summary of the Changes:
   After the geometryUtil class updates (#399), there were some tests that were not passing on MacOS, mainly the OS-dependent bounds for certain notes; use case dependency edge; implicit parameter nodes, self call edges, and call nodes in sequence diagrams etc. To fix, I went through the tester, and if the change in bound was like 1 pixel, I just changed it. If it was more, I checked the diagram to make sure it rendered properly and if so, I changed it.
   Most of the bound changes actually matched that for Windows, and if not, become significantly closer. The worst case discrepancy went from +- 20 pixels to +- 5 pixels.

MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0MzY4MzM1MzYx.Title: #140 Attach or Detach selected nodes with shift key (ClassDiagram)
MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0MzY4MzM1MzYx.Body:
**Summary of Changes**

- Add the `ShiftKeyPressedHandler` in the `.gui` package to facillitate key handling.
- Delegate the method call of `keyPressed` in the `DiagramTab` class to the `DiagramCanvasController` class.
- Add the Attach and Detach Commands and their preconditions: `canAttachToPackage` and `canDetachFromPackage`.
- Encapsulate the concrete implementation of the attachement commands in `ClassDiagramBuilder`.

**Achieved Performance**

- If the parent of the selected nodes is null, add the nodes to the package node under the position of the first node in the selected nodes.
- If the parent of the selected nodes is not null, detach the nodes from their current parent. Attach the detached nodes to the parent of their current parent if there exists such a node; otherwise attach them directly to the Diagram.
- Otherwise, do nothing.

**Design Concerns**

- The current node attachment operation only applies to Class Diagram. Might want to generalize the methods implementation if we want to add package nodes in other types of Diagram.
- I encapsulate the implementation of node attachment methods in `ClassDiagramBuilder` because other Diagrams do not have Package nodes and the type hierarchy of `ChildNode` may also cause NullPointerException (e.g. try to detach the `FieldNode` from its parent in Object Diagram)
- I would add tests for the methods after you review and agree with the design choices.
MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0MzY4MzM1MzYx.Author: cc-21

Comments.Body: I noticed that in the Class Diagram, the only type of `ParentNode` is `PackageNode`, which is also a `ChildNode`. Therefore, the code `ParentNode outerParent = (pParent instanceof ChildNode)? ((ChildNode)pParent).getParent():null;` in the method `createDetachFromPackageOperation` is unnecessary since if `pParent` is a Parent Node, it must be a Package Node.
Comments.Author: cc-21

Comments.Body: Maybe we could change the code to `ParentNode outerParent = ((ChildNode)pParent).getParent();`. The previous line of code applies if we want to add the package node feature to other types of diagram.
Comments.Author: cc-21

Comments.Body:
I also wonder if I could initialize the nodes I used in the test class before all tests.
I noticed that the nodes used in the tests are quite common and I want to avoid initializing new nodes repetitively in each test. Currently, I kept the node name as node1, 2, 3, etc. I would like to change them to package1, classNode1 etc.
Comments.Author: cc-21

PR_kwDOAblrlc43nC9-.Title: Add note about OS package managers
PR_kwDOAblrlc43nC9-.Body:
If your package manager has JetUML in its repos, getting the software that way may be preferable to manually downloading and updating it.

I've been maintaining a [build script](https://aur.archlinux.org/cgit/aur.git/tree/PKGBUILD?h=jetuml) for the thin jar on the [AUR](https://wiki.archlinux.org/title/Arch_User_Repository) for two years.[^1]

It seems there was also work on getting JetUML onto flathub #433, so imo it'd be useful to list these places in the docs.

[^1]: Basically this means that users of Arch based Distros can get JetUML like any other package. On installation, a script downloads the latest jar from your GitHub releases and creates desktop shortcuts and scripts that will call Java with the correct parameters to launch the app.
PR_kwDOAblrlc43nC9-.Author: benthillerkus

PR_kwDOAblrlc5hIOAZ.Title: Edge Constraint Classification + Feedback System
PR_kwDOAblrlc5hIOAZ.Body:
This pull request turns edge constraints from methods to separate classes.

When an edge constraint is not satisfied during semantic validation, the validation is stopped and an error toast is displayed with the text associated to the edge constraint in `JetUML.properties`.

The `NotificationHandler` class has been renamed `NotificationService` and now has more information and its lifecycle (especially the initialization of its required attributes).

It also contains new and updated tests to account for the changes/additions to the code.
PR_kwDOAblrlc5hIOAZ.Author: ArthusWQZ

If it was more, I checked the diagram to make sure it rendered properly and if so, I changed it.

PR_kwDOAblrlc4xGa0m.Title: Issue#437 Fix get co-removals for sequence diagrams
PR_kwDOAblrlc4xGa0m.Body:
Changes were made to fix issue #437, ensuring that when the user deletes a `ConstructorEdge`, downstream `ImplicitParameterNode` objects are not deleted, as well. 

To fix this, a conditional statement was added to `SequenceDiagramBuilder.getCoRemovals()` which checks if the input diagram element is a `ConstructorEdge`. If so, downstream `ImplicitParameterNode` objects are removed from the list to be returned. A new test was written to consider the behavior of `getCoRemovals()` with a `ConstructorEdge`. An unrelated annotation error was also fixed in `TestControlFlow`. 

**Here is an example scenario:**

Before applying this fix:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/90351737/149640047-a311160f-4659-4dc9-9975-c8cf67914b13.png)

After fixing applying this fix:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/90351737/149639988-b02e5c0d-779f-4be8-93a7-17515ab5a5ba.png)
PR_kwDOAblrlc4xGa0m.Author: maddy0218

MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0NDM2MDY1MjM0.Title: #353 Modify the tweakOrder method
MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0NDM2MDY1MjM0.Body:
**Summary of Changes**

Modify the comparison logic in the `tweakOrder` method, for nodes that have parents:

1. If two nodes have the same parent, place the latter child node at the front.
2. If two nodes have different parents, order the nodes according to the positions of their parent nodes in `rootNodes` of the diagram.

PR_kwDOAblrlc5m4bFj.Title: [#524] Taskbar Flashing Bug Fix
PR_kwDOAblrlc5m4bFj.Body:
This PR is a simple fix for the flashing bug mentioned in #524.

The use of `Stage::close` with JavaFX when the window is minimized makes it flash on the taskbar.
Therefore, notifications are not closed anymore once they reach the end of their lifespan.

They are moved to another list, `aDeadNotifications`, and all the notifications in this list are closed and removed when a new notification is spawned. I assumed that notifications are only spawned when the window is maximized (since they are all based on user actions).
PR_kwDOAblrlc5m4bFj.Author: ArthusWQZ

PR_kwDOAblrlc42o4NP.Title: #375 Add EdgePath, EdgeStorage, and EdgePriority
PR_kwDOAblrlc42o4NP.Body:
**In order to plan the paths of edges in class diagrams, several initial changes are needed:**
1. A way to represent the path of an edge
2. A way to store the paths of edges
3. A way to classify edges so that their paths can be planned in a predictable order

To meet these requirements 3 classes: `EdgePath`, `EdgeStorage`, and `EdgePriority` were added.

### **Representing and storing the paths of edges**
An `EdgePath` class was added, which represents the path of an edge in a class diagram as a list of `Point`s. An `EdgeStorage` class was created, which maps the edges of a class diagram to `EdgePath`s. 

Once the `Layouter` class is implemented, it will plan the `EdgePath`s of edges in a class diagram and store them in `EdgeStorage`. Then, these stored paths can be used to draw clean, readable class diagrams without edge overlapping. 

![edgestorage](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/90351737/164767646-3dcaa10f-3b9e-4688-ac58-c83cab025f77.jpg)

The `EdgeStorage` class has several public methods, including a method which allows users to store an `Edge` and its `EdgePath`, and a method to get the stored `EdgePath` of an `Edge`. Notably, `EdgeStorage` has a `clearStorage()` method which clears all entries of edges and their `EdgePath`s. This is necessary because `EdgeStorage` must be cleared and refilled whenever the diagram changes.

### **Establishing a priority classification for edges**
An enumerated type `EdgePriority` was added which classifies class diagram edges. This way, all edges of a higher `EdgePriority` are planned before edges of lower `EdgePriority`. Classifying edges using an enumerated type is less error-prone than using the Edge class hierarchy. For example, Inheritance and Implementation edges are both considered `GeneralizationEdges`, and many different edges can be self-edges. The `EdgePriority` enumerated type has several public methods which are useful for edge
classification.

The order of EdgePriority is as follows (where Inheritance edges have the highest priority):
1. Inheritance
5. Implementation
6. Aggregation
7. Composition
8. Association
9. Dependency
10. Self-edges
11. Other (non-class diagram edges)

Tests for `EdgePath`, `EdgePriority`, and `EdgeStorage` were also added.
PR_kwDOAblrlc42o4NP.Author: maddy0218

PR_kwDOAblrlc4uybMW.Title: #446 Improvements to Edge Label Alignment and Layout
PR_kwDOAblrlc4uybMW.Body:
The line lengths of LabeledStraightEdges, SegmentedEdges, and StateTransitionEdges is now determined dynamically based on the horizontal and vertical distances between the two points that the segment relates. During this code addition, it was noticed that some code for StateTransitionEdge label wrapping was unreachable so it was removed. 

The labels of horizontal segments now hover above the arrowhead to match the behaviour of vertical segment labels. 

The labels of SegmentedEdges no longer overlaps with the segments themselves. Logic was added to the relevant method to be able to differentiate between a "step up" segment and a "step down" segment. Before the modifications of this pull request, the behaviour was different for two same nodes connected by an edge when the nodes were inverted left to right in the diagram. 

Unit tests were added to cover all lines of code added. 

Demo for class diagram:

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/43301868/142680455-647895e7-ab60-4190-8c3f-7de73e2c7668.mov

Demo for state diagram: 

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/43301868/142680445-14a55205-7b3c-4431-85ea-8c4e5b2de85b.mov
PR_kwDOAblrlc4uybMW.Author: delphinepilon

D_kwDOAblrlc4AXvWV.Title: Upcoming support for underlining and italics in classes
D_kwDOAblrlc4AXvWV.Body:
The next release will support underlining and italicizing text in class diagrams. This will enable creating diagrams that are even closer to the UML standard (with abstract elements in italics and static elements underlined). 

Consistent with JetUML's purpose as a lightweight and fast UML sketching tool, the text decorations are activated through simple markup in the free-form text for model elements. 

![image](https://github.com/prmr/JetUML/assets/6033492/12b418d6-5632-4782-a01b-c7f2f03da3cb)
D_kwDOAblrlc4AXvWV.Author: prmr

D_kwDOAblrlc4Aa6FN.Title: Release 3.8 is out!
D_kwDOAblrlc4Aa6FN.Body: JetUML now features a dark mode, support for italics and underlines in class names, an enhanced user manual, and more. Thanks to @jkcoding7 for the great work!
D_kwDOAblrlc4Aa6FN.Author: prmr
